# Tekla.Structures.Drawing

Auto-generated from vendor docs for tekla 2025.0. 341 types in this namespace.

## AlongLineOrWithLeaderLineAndParentObjectAlongPartPlacingType (class)

The AlongLineOrWithLeaderLineAndParentObjectAlongPartPlacingType class defines a placing type that places the object using the along line placing as the first option. If there is no room for the first option the fallback option will be used. The fallback option is placing the object with a leader line and rotated to be at the same angle as the connected part.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1abcb5b7-e5df-cccf-642e-fef8e6c1d34c)

### Constructors
- `public AlongLineOrWithLeaderLineAndParentObjectAlongPartPlacingType()` — Creates a new along line or with leader line and parent object along part placing type instance.

## AlongLineOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType (class)

The AlongLineOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType class defines a placing type that places the object using the along line placing as the first option. If there is no room for the first option the fallback option will be used. The fallback option is placing the object with a leader line.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/71b65a9e-490c-8fad-d0f6-73e58516134a)

### Constructors
- `public AlongLineOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType()` — Creates a new along line or with leader line placing type instance.

## AlongLinePlacing (class)

The AlongLinePlacing class defines a placing where the object is located and moved along a line defined by two points.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/add4e174-6dc4-3eeb-7b1f-2d013882190d)

### Constructors
- `public AlongLinePlacing(Point StartPoint, Point EndPoint)` — Creates a new along line placing instance. The given two points define the line to be used for the placing.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a2360f3f-0551-3a34-e20d-40e9168f3c6b)

### Properties
- `EndPoint` (Point, get/set) — The end point of the line that the object is placed on.
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — The start point of the line that the object is placed on.

## AlongLinePlacingType (class)

The AlongLinePlacingType class defines a placing type that places the object using the along line placing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/7c46b8e9-1452-4a67-55c3-ed71c540f360)

### Constructors
- `public AlongLinePlacingType()` — Creates a new along line placing type instance.

## AlongPartCenteredPlacingType (class)

The AlongPartCenteredPlacingType class defines a placing type that places the object using the along line placing. The line used will be the part's edge in the center of that line.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/297035d0-7383-a0c6-3c0a-c8f868b7834b)

### Constructors
- `public AlongPartCenteredPlacingType()` — Creates a new along part centered placing type instance.

## AngleDimension (class)

The AngleDimension class defines an angle dimension between two lines. The dimension can be shown by a triangle or in degrees. You can also enter the triangle base length.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/31a8523d-decf-f1ff-75e6-b69b16d35454)

### Constructors
- `public AngleDimension(ViewBase View, Point Origin, Point Point1, Point Point2, double Distance)` — Creates a new angle dimension instance using three points.
- `public AngleDimension(ViewBase View, Point Origin, Vector Vector1, Vector Vector2, double Distance)` — Creates a new angle dimension instance using the origin and two vectors relative to the origin.
- `public AngleDimension(ViewBase View, Point Origin, Point Point1, Point Point2, double Distance, AngleDimensionAttributes Attributes)` — Creates a new angle dimension instance using three points.
- `public AngleDimension(ViewBase View, Point Origin, Vector Vector1, Vector Vector2, double Distance, AngleDimensionAttributes Attributes)` — Creates a new angle dimension instance using the origin and two vectors relative to the origin.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (AngleDimensionAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle dimension attributes.
- `Distance` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the distance (from the origin to the second point's direction). The distance is measured in paper millimeters.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `Origin` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the origin of the dimension.
- `Point1` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the first point.
- `Point2` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the second point.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## AngleDimensionAttributes (class)

The AngleDimensionAttributes class contains the attributes for angle dimensions.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9eb486dc-e9c5-8674-7844-e74d53d50333)

### Properties
- `Arrowhead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead attributes of the dimension.
- `Color` (DrawingColors, get/set) — The color of the dimension line.
- `Format` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionFormatAttributes, get/set) — The format attributes of the dimension.
- `Placing` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionPlacingAttributes, get/set) — The placing attributes of the dimension.
- `Text` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTextAttributes, get/set) — The text attributes of the dimension.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — The dimension text background transparency. (transparent/opaque)
- `TriangleBase` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the triangle base length which controls the base dimension shown for bevel dimensions (only if Type is AngleTypes.Triangle).
- `TrueColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (RGB enabled) of the dimension line.
- `Type` (AngleTypes, get/set) — The type of the angle.

## AngleTypes (enum)

The different types for angle dimensions.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/62aa1446-baf4-d2f9-326e-a1a502f11e66)

### Values
- `AngleOnSide` = `1` — Shows the angle dimensions in degrees on the side.
- `AngleAtVertex` = `3` — Shows the angle dimensions in degrees at the angle vertex.
- `Triangle` = `2` — Shows the angle dimensions using a triangle. The triangle base length can be configured.
- `TriangleWithDegrees` = `4` — Shows the angle using a triangle, also shows the angle in degrees on one side of the triangle.
- `AngleOnSideGradian` = `5` — Shows the angle dimensions in gradians on the side.
- `AngleAtVertexGradian` = `6` — Shows the angle dimensions in gradians at the angle vertex.
- `TriangleWithGradians` = `7` — Shows the angle using a triangle, also shows the angle in gradians on one side of the triangle.

## Arc (class)

The Arc class defines an arc that is a two-point line with an optional curve radius. It can be created using either two points and a radius or three points.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/20ac2e4d-1c1f-23f7-1cfa-3d94717b1751)

### Constructors
- `public Arc(ViewBase inView, Point inPoint1, Point inPoint2, double inRadius)` — Creates a new arc instance with standard attributes and the given two points and radius.
- `public Arc(ViewBase inView, Point inPoint1, Point inPoint2, Point inPoint3)` — Creates a new arc instance with standard attributes and the given three points.
- `public Arc(ViewBase inView, Point inPoint1, Point inPoint2, double inRadius, Arc.ArcAttributes inAttributes)` — Creates a new arc instance with the given two points, radius and specified attributes.
- `public Arc(ViewBase inView, Point StartPoint, Point EndPoint, Point CenterPoint, Arc.ArcAttributes inAttributes)` — Creates a new arc instance with the given three points and specified attributes.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (Arc.ArcAttributes, get/set) — The attributes of the arc.
- `EndPoint` (Point, get/set) — The end point of the arc.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `Radius` (Double, get/set) — The radius of the arc.
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — The start point of the arc.

## Arc.ArcAttributes (class)

The ArcAttributes class is the attributes class for the arc.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/fe75912d-5edb-8618-bff7-26101e15bcca)

### Constructors
- `public ArcAttributes()` — Creates a new default arc attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public ArcAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new arc attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4487b421-12ff-7f14-e459-d8007696ea56)

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/e57b493d-61b1-036c-333f-0c0a8610d197)

### Properties
- `Arrowhead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead attributes of the open graphic object. The end points of an open graphic object can contain arrowheads.
- `Line` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line attributes of the graphic object. A graphic object always consists of lines.

## ArrowheadAttributes (class)

The ArrowheadAttributes class handles attributes related to arrowheads of different objects.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/00085909-c077-e32b-79d7-fa0e543b5826)

### Constructors
- `public ArrowheadAttributes()` — Creates a new default arrowhead attributes instance. The default values are ArrowheadPositions.None, ArrowheadTypes.FilledArrow, height 2.0 and width 1.0.
- `public ArrowheadAttributes(ArrowheadPositions arrowPosition, ArrowheadTypes head, double height, double width)` — Creates a new arrowhead attributes instance with the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1cf44e40-562e-14b3-74c4-d023a20009fa)

### Properties
- `ArrowPosition` (ArrowheadPositions, get/set) — The position of the arrowhead. The position is always ArrowheadPositions.Both for dimensions.
- `Head` (ArrowheadTypes, get/set) — The type of the arrowhead.
- `Height` (Double, get/set) — The height of the arrowhead.
- `Width` (Double, get/set) — The width of the arrowhead.

## ArrowheadPositions (enum)

The positions of the arrowheads on the line.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/24eef0f3-f2ad-7860-664a-7bc488a78dfb)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — No arrowheads on the line.
- `Start` = `1` — One arrowhead at the start of the line.
- `End` = `2` — One arrowhead at the end of the line.
- `Both` = `3` — Arrowheads at both ends of the line.

## ArrowheadTypes (enum)

The arrowhead types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/102571cd-edbe-ea94-45dd-f3fd265f745d)

### Values
- `NoArrow` = `-1005` — No arrowhead.
- `FilledArrow` = `-1004` — The filled arrowhead.
- `LineArrow` = `-1003` — The line arrowhead.
- `CircleArrow` = `-1002` — The open circle arrowhead.
- `FilledCircleArrow` = `-1001` — The filled circle arrowhead.

## AssemblyDrawing (class)

The AssemblyDrawing class is for handling assembly drawings.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c03052ca-3759-02d5-95d9-5d89b274e4ad)

### Constructors
- `public AssemblyDrawing(Identifier assemblyIdentifier)` — Instantiates a new assembly drawing with standard attributes.
- `public AssemblyDrawing(Identifier assemblyIdentifier, int sheetNumber)` — Instantiates a new assembly drawing with standard attributes and with a specified sheet number.
- `public AssemblyDrawing(Identifier assemblyIdentifier, string AttributeFile)` — Instantiates a new assembly drawing with given attributes.
- `public AssemblyDrawing(Identifier assemblyIdentifier, int sheetNumber, string AttributeFile)` — Instantiates a new assembly drawing with given attributes and with a specified sheet number.

### Methods
#### `public bool CommitChanges()`

Commits the changes made to the drawings database so far. Commit inserts a kind of save-point into the database and the save-point will be retrieved if the user does undo. Commit also executes all messages. Please note that you need to also to save the drawings database to persists the changes to disk.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/bc26a611-bd6a-535a-4461-943b8bf5351c)

#### `public bool CommitChanges(string Message)`

Commits the changes made to the drawings database so far. Commit inserts a kind of save-point into the database and the save-point will be retrieved if the user does undo. Commit also executes all messages. Please note that you need to also to save the drawings database to persists the changes to disk.

**Parameters:**
- `Message` (System.String) — The message to be saved with the commit.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/92109855-5313-7446-bef4-ab6dde6875f1)

#### `public override bool Delete()`

Deletes the drawing. An active drawing cannot be deleted.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4bcff604-27c4-487f-bb7d-5d80a2d87d8b)

### Properties
- `AssemblyIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The identifier of the model object assembly.
- `CommitMessage` (String, get) — Gets the commit message
- `CreationDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing creation date.
- `DrawingTypeStr` (String, get) — The type of the drawing
- `IsFrozen` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is frozen.
- `IsIssued` (Boolean, get) — Describes whether the drawing is issued.
- `IsIssuedButModified` (Boolean, get) — Describes whether the drawing is issued but modified since it was issued.
- `IsLocked` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is locked.
- `IsLockedBy` (String, get) — Gets the logged in username that set the drawing to locked
- `IsMasterDrawing` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is a master drawing (shown with a special symbol on the drawing list).
- `IsReadyForIssue` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is ready for issue.
- `IsReadyForIssueBy` (String, get) — Gets the logged in username that set the drawing Ready for issue
- `IssuingDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing issuing date.
- `Layout` (LayoutAttributes, get) — Gets the drawing layout attributes.
- `Mark` (String, get) — The drawing mark.
- `ModificationDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing modification date.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the drawing.
- `OutputDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing output date. XS_DISABLE_DRAWING_PLOT_DATE controls whether this date is set.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `SectionViewStartLabel` (String, get) — Gets the drawing attribute for start label of section view
- `SheetNumber` (Int32, get) — The sheet number of the drawing.
- `Title1` (String, get/set) — The first drawing title.
- `Title2` (String, get/set) — The second drawing title.
- `Title3` (String, get/set) — The third drawing title.
- `UpToDateStatus` (DrawingUpToDateStatus, get) — Gets the drawing up to date status.

## AttributesBase (class)

The AttributesBase class is the class that all the main attributes that can load attribute files inherit from.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0bdac29d-037b-476a-e615-bac587510903)

### Methods
#### `public abstract bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/da11fa4f-a98d-c770-d0e2-f458bc3b82dc)

#### `public abstract bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the specified file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file. Use only the filename, not the extension.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5597a575-45e2-aa83-6576-390f23af3e48)

## AutoSizeOptions (enum)

Options for controlling how the sheet size is calculated when the SizeDefinitionMode is AutoSize.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/84383201-3a42-4585-f98b-8f150ecfcb89)

### Values
- `CalculatedSizes` = `0` — Use "calculated size" values of the selected layout in the drawing.
- `FixedSizes` = `1` — Use "fixed size" values of the selected layout in the drawing.
- `CalculatedAndFixedSizes` = `2` — Use both "fixed size" and "calculated size" values of the selected layout in the drawing.

## BaseLinePlacing (class)

The BaseLinePlacing class defines a placing where the object is placed using a line as a reference. This will thus define the object's X-axis along that line.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d8a3f20c-80e0-b943-0b1f-1ab47b62fcb5)

### Constructors
- `public BaseLinePlacing(Point StartPoint, Point EndPoint)` — Creates a new base line placing instance. The given two points define the line to be used for the placing.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/e9a8dbbb-56e4-be2a-1271-32945e7a22de)

### Properties
- `EndPoint` (Point, get/set) — Sets the end point of the line that the object is using for the placement.
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — Sets the start point of the line that the object is using for the placement.

## BaseLinePlacingType (class)

The BaseLinePlacingType class defines a placing type that places the object using the base line placing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/31d87564-ac2c-c0ca-f3a6-b0a7ff9ef1a4)

### Constructors
- `public BaseLinePlacingType()` — Creates a new base line placing type instance.

## BaseLineWithArrowAtEndPointPlacingType (class)

The BaseLineWithArrowAtEndPointPlacingType class defines a placing type that places the object using the base line placing. The line is drawn and will have an arrow at the end point.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5a0a60b1-8b68-6762-6183-f1a3e130a7e2)

### Constructors
- `public BaseLineWithArrowAtEndPointPlacingType()` — Creates a new base line with arrow at end point placing type instance.

## BaseLineWithArrowAtStartPointPlacingType (class)

The BaseLineWithArrowAtStartPointPlacingType class defines a placing type that places the object using the base line placing. The line is drawn and will have an arrow at the start point.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/221c5978-602c-a603-92f8-78b46dc1ebb3)

### Constructors
- `public BaseLineWithArrowAtStartPointPlacingType()` — Creates a new base line with arrow at start point placing type instance.

## Bolt (class)

The Bolt class contains methods related to bolts. A bolt is a drawing object derived from a model object. It represents a drawing bolt that has a reference to the actual bolt in the model database.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/3ab42a97-20a2-22b1-8039-cc1d67963b41)

### Methods
#### `public override bool Delete()`

Calls the system to delete the object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

#### `public bool GetDoubleUserProperties(out Dictionary<string, double> values)`

### Properties
- `Attributes` (Bolt.BoltAttributes, get/set) — The attributes of the bolt. For more information see Bolt.BoltAttributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## Bolt.BoltAttributes (class)

The BoltAttributes class is the attributes class for the bolt.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/7edfee41-9b01-8791-1650-e4b2e91a169b)

### Constructors
- `public BoltAttributes()` — Creates a new default bolt attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public BoltAttributes(string AttributeFile)` — Creates a new bolt attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/54817577-3916-4204-aee7-4746a827260f)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/544b8092-9dc3-ef98-1b98-6c8c619f4714)

### Properties
- `Color` (DrawingColors, get/set) — The color of the bolt.
- `CustomPresentation` (String, get/set) — Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the bolt
- `Representation` (Bolt.Representation, get/set) — The representation type of the bolt.
- `SymbolContainsAxis` (Boolean, get/set) — True if the bolt symbol contains an axis.
- `SymbolContainsHole` (Boolean, get/set) — True if the bolt symbol contains a hole.
- `TrueColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (RGB enabled) of the bolt.

## Bolt.Representation (enum)

The bolt representations.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d7923fba-2a16-e1e1-262f-82e3b4fe739c)

### Values
- `Solid` = `1` — The solid representation.
- `ExactSolid` = `9` — The exact solid representation.
- `Symbol` = `2` — The symbol representation.
- `Symbol2` = `4` — The second symbol representation.
- `Symbol3` = `5` — The third symbol representation.
- `DINSymbol` = `7` — The DIN symbol representation.
- `UserDefined` = `8` — The user defined representation.

## CannotCreateSectionViewFrom3dView (class)

## CastUnitDrawing (class)

The CastUnitDrawing class is for handling cast unit drawings.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4bdbe4f7-156c-9d02-1b17-8b6423b2b501)

### Constructors
- `public CastUnitDrawing(Identifier castUnitIdentifier)` — Instantiates a new cast unit drawing with standard attributes for a given cast unit.
- `public CastUnitDrawing(Identifier castUnitIdentifier, int sheetNumber)` — Instantiates a new cast unit drawing with standard attributes and with a specified sheet number for a given cast unit.
- `public CastUnitDrawing(Identifier castUnitIdentifier, string AttributeFile)` — Instantiates a new cast unit drawing with given attributes for a given cast unit.
- `public CastUnitDrawing(Identifier castUnitIdentifier, CastUnitDrawing.CastUnitDrawingCreationType castUnitDrawingCreationType)` — Instantiates a new cast unit drawing with standard attributes for a given cast unit.
- `public CastUnitDrawing(Identifier castUnitIdentifier, int sheetNumber, string AttributeFile)` — Instantiates a new cast unit drawing with given attributes and with a specified sheet number for a given cast unit.
- `public CastUnitDrawing(Identifier castUnitIdentifier, CastUnitDrawing.CastUnitDrawingCreationType castUnitDrawingCreationType, int sheetNumber)` — Instantiates a new cast unit drawing by id with standard attributes and with a specified sheet number for a given cast unit.
- `public CastUnitDrawing(Identifier castUnitIdentifier, CastUnitDrawing.CastUnitDrawingCreationType castUnitDrawingCreationType, string AttributeFile)` — Instantiates a new cast unit drawing with given attributes for a given cast unit.
- `public CastUnitDrawing(Identifier castUnitIdentifier, CastUnitDrawing.CastUnitDrawingCreationType castUnitDrawingCreationType, int sheetNumber, string AttributeFile)` — Instantiates a new cast unit drawing by id with given attributes and with a specified sheet number for a given cast unit.

### Methods
#### `public bool CommitChanges()`

Commits the changes made to the drawings database so far. Commit inserts a kind of save-point into the database and the save-point will be retrieved if the user does undo. Commit also executes all messages. Please note that you need to also to save the drawings database to persists the changes to disk.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/bc26a611-bd6a-535a-4461-943b8bf5351c)

#### `public bool CommitChanges(string Message)`

Commits the changes made to the drawings database so far. Commit inserts a kind of save-point into the database and the save-point will be retrieved if the user does undo. Commit also executes all messages. Please note that you need to also to save the drawings database to persists the changes to disk.

**Parameters:**
- `Message` (System.String) — The message to be saved with the commit.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/92109855-5313-7446-bef4-ab6dde6875f1)

#### `public bool PlaceViews()`

Places views on the sheet. Computes the required size of the views to fit the parts, then places the views so that they fit on the sheet and do not overlap. Adjusts the sheet size if needed.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

### Properties
- `CastUnitById` (Boolean, get) — Cast unit definition method. True means the cast unit definition method is by cast unit id. False means the cast unit definition method is by cast unit position.
- `CastUnitIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The identifier of the model object cast unit.
- `CommitMessage` (String, get) — Gets the commit message
- `CreationDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing creation date.
- `DrawingTypeStr` (String, get) — The type of the drawing
- `IsFrozen` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is frozen.
- `IsIssued` (Boolean, get) — Describes whether the drawing is issued.
- `IsIssuedButModified` (Boolean, get) — Describes whether the drawing is issued but modified since it was issued.
- `IsLocked` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is locked.
- `IsLockedBy` (String, get) — Gets the logged in username that set the drawing to locked
- `IsMasterDrawing` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is a master drawing (shown with a special symbol on the drawing list).
- `IsReadyForIssue` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is ready for issue.
- `IsReadyForIssueBy` (String, get) — Gets the logged in username that set the drawing Ready for issue
- `IssuingDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing issuing date.
- `Layout` (LayoutAttributes, get) — Gets the drawing layout attributes.
- `Mark` (String, get) — The drawing mark.
- `ModificationDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing modification date.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the drawing.
- `OutputDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing output date. XS_DISABLE_DRAWING_PLOT_DATE controls whether this date is set.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `SectionViewStartLabel` (String, get) — Gets the drawing attribute for start label of section view
- `SheetNumber` (Int32, get) — The sheet number of the drawing.
- `Title1` (String, get/set) — The first drawing title.
- `Title2` (String, get/set) — The second drawing title.
- `Title3` (String, get/set) — The third drawing title.
- `UpToDateStatus` (DrawingUpToDateStatus, get) — Gets the drawing up to date status.

## CastUnitDrawing.CastUnitDrawingCreationType (enum)

Defines the Cast Unit Drawing Creation type.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9c093d34-dd10-12ed-df8a-b676bf0e32f2)

### Values
- `CastUnitDrawingByPosition` = `0` — Creating the Cast Unit drawing by Position means we follow the position of the cast unit.
- `CastUnitDrawingById` = `1` — Creating the Cast Unit drawing by Id means we follow the id of the cast unit.

## Circle (class)

The Circle class defines the graphical object circle. A circle can also be hatched.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/50243dfc-c212-a805-ac2e-dee4eead998c)

### Constructors
- `public Circle(ViewBase view, Point centerPoint, double radius)` — Creates a new circle instance with a center point and a radius using standard attributes.
- `public Circle(ViewBase view, Point centerPoint, double radius, Circle.CircleAttributes attributes)` — Creates a new circle instance with a center point and a radius using the given attributes.

- `Line` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line attributes of the graphic object. A graphic object always consists of lines.

## ClosedGraphicObject (class)

## ClosedGraphicObject.ClosedGraphicObjectAttributes (class)

The ClosedGraphicObjectAttributes class is the attributes class for the closed graphic object.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/97faf0f7-b5ad-77df-42a3-50ed1b6c5a6b)

### Methods
#### `public abstract bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/da11fa4f-a98d-c770-d0e2-f458bc3b82dc)

#### `public abstract bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the specified file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file. Use only the filename, not the extension.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5597a575-45e2-aa83-6576-390f23af3e48)

### Properties
- `BehindModelObjects` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets whether the closed graphic object is drawn behind model objects
- `Hatch` (GraphicObjectHatchAttributes, get/set) — The hatch attributes of the closed graphic object (the closed graphic object is hatchable).
- `Line` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line attributes of the graphic object. A graphic object always consists of lines.

## Cloud (class)

The Cloud class defines a cloud that is a polygon with a specific size set for the bulges.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/57002cc6-3302-3201-156a-658298f9a70f)

### Constructors
- `public Cloud(ViewBase view, PointList pointList)` — Creates a new cloud instance with a point list using standard attributes.
- `public Cloud(ViewBase view, PointList pointList, Cloud.CloudAttributes attributes)` — Creates a new cloud instance with a point list using the given attributes.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (Cloud.CloudAttributes, get/set) — The attributes for the cloud.
- `Bulge` (Double, get/set) — The bulge (curve) to use for the cloud (a bulge is the ratio between the height and width of an object).
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `Points` (PointList, get/set) — The list of points for the cloud. The points you insert will specify the corner points of the cloud. After you insert the object, you will get every cloud point back.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## Cloud.CloudAttributes (class)

The CloudAttributes class is the attributes class for the cloud.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c0fcd085-a3df-192b-9afe-5328bbc2b9d3)

#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

- `Hatch` (GraphicObjectHatchAttributes, get/set) — The hatch attributes of the closed graphic object (the closed graphic object is hatchable).
- `Line` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line attributes of the graphic object. A graphic object always consists of lines.

## Connection (class)

The Connection class contains methods related to connections. A connection is a drawing object derived from a model object. It represents a drawing connection that has a reference to the actual connection in the model database.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4d614ec4-28d4-f693-d727-7821825cbc54)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (AttributesBase, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes of the current object. Only attributes of the same type as the object are allowed.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## ContainerElement (class)

The ContainerElement class defines container elements. A container element is a mark element that has a frame and contains other mark elements. A container element may also contain container elements to achieve nested frames.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a922aaee-1a0a-7083-3f39-496dff2e4641)

### Constructors
- `public ContainerElement()` — Initializes a new instance of the ContainerElement class

### Methods
#### `public void Add(ElementBase value)`

Adds a new element base to the end of the list.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.ElementBase) — The element base to be added to the list.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/01ce5ad6-2d97-4dba-7a04-6459a4476c13)

#### `public void Clear()`

Removes all elements from the container instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/3ebf4c11-e7c8-6443-e915-da0b4ce362f8)

#### `public override Object Clone()`

Clones the object.

**Returns:** `Object` — A copy of the object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/8c2b52ff-4f86-62b0-0f43-3ea9f9abbc41)

#### `public void CopyTo(Array array, int index)`

Copies the elements of the ICollection to an Array, starting at a particular Array index.

**Parameters:**
- `array` (System.Array) — The one-dimensional Array that is the destination of the elements copied from the ICollection. The Array must have zero-based indexing.
- `index` (System.Int32) — The zero-based index in the array at which copying begins.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/25d92102-0435-213f-88b4-75f7e22a74fc)

#### `public IEnumerator GetEnumerator()`

Returns an enumerator that iterates through a collection.

**Returns:** `IEnumerator` — An IEnumerator object that can be used to iterate through the collection.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/fcecd7e0-80e1-bce3-3c16-0132ee1f7d1e)

#### `public override string GetUnformattedString()`

Returns the content as a string without formatting.

**Returns:** `String` — The content as a string without formatting.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a7f3aff9-b3d9-5fb4-3044-7937d3719ed7)

#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/970e9be2-ffcc-8465-8a29-8f5d872deb90)

### Properties
- `Count` (Int32, get) — Gets the number of elements contained in the ICollection.
- `Frame` (Frame, get/set) — The frame of the container element.
- `IsSynchronized` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether access to the ICollection is synchronized (thread safe).
- `SyncRoot` (Object, get) — Gets an object that can be used to synchronize access to the ICollection.

## ContainerView (class)

The ContainerView class contains container views that contain other drawing objects, including other views and container views. The drawing sheet is a container view. Container views do not contain drawing representations of model objects.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b688c316-8997-c314-172a-f0663ad1def1)

### Methods
#### `public override bool Delete()`

Deletes the view and its children objects from the database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (AttributesBase, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes of the current object. Only attributes of the same type as the object are allowed.
- `ExtremaCenter` (Point, get) — Gets the view extrema center.
- `FrameOrigin` (Vector, get/set) — The vector from the view origin to the frame origin.
- `Height` (Double, get/set) — The height of the view frame in paper coordinates.
- `IsSheet` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the container view is the drawing's sheet.
- `Origin` (Point, get/set) — The view origin coordinates in the sheet.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `Width` (Double, get/set) — The width of the view frame in paper coordinates.

## CurvedDimensionOrthogonal (class)

The CurvedDimensionOrthogonal class defines a curved dimension with orthogonal reference lines.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/acd27e09-3557-4ceb-9755-c78b11ec20bb)

### Constructors
- `public CurvedDimensionOrthogonal(ViewBase View, Point ArcPoint1, Point ArcPoint2, Point ArcPoint3, Point StartPoint, Point EndPoint, double Distance)` — Creates a new curved dimension with orthogonal reference lines using default attributes.
- `public CurvedDimensionOrthogonal(ViewBase View, Point ArcPoint1, Point ArcPoint2, Point ArcPoint3, Point StartPoint, Point EndPoint, double Distance, CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonal.CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonalAttributes Attributes)` — Creates a new curved dimension with orthogonal reference lines.

## CurvedDimensionRadial (class)

The CurvedDimensionRadial class defines a curved dimension with radial reference points. The dimension text on the line can be either a distance or an angle value.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/061b94cd-8971-4993-a437-736478c0a124)

### Constructors
- `public CurvedDimensionRadial(ViewBase View, Point ArcPoint1, Point ArcPoint2, Point ArcPoint3, Point StartPoint, Point EndPoint, double Distance)` — Creates a new curved dimension with radial reference points using standard attributes.
- `public CurvedDimensionRadial(ViewBase View, Point ArcPoint1, Point ArcPoint2, Point ArcPoint3, Point StartPoint, Point EndPoint, double Distance, CurvedDimensionSetRadial.CurvedDimensionSetRadialAttributes Attributes)` — Creates a new curved dimension with radial reference points.

- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — The start point of the dimension.

## CurvedDimensionSetBase (class)

The CurvedDimensionSetBase class is the base class for curved dimension sets which are defined by an arc and a point list.

- `ArcPoint1` (Point, get) — Gets the first arc point.
- `ArcPoint2` (Point, get) — Gets the second arc point.
- `ArcPoint3` (Point, get) — Gets the third arc point.
- `Attributes` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the dimension set attributes.
- `DimensionPoints` (PointList, get) — Gets a list of the dimension points.
- `Distance` (Double, get) — Gets the distance. The distance is measured in paper millimeters.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## CurvedDimensionSetBase.CurvedDimensionSetBaseAttributes (class)

The CurvedDimensionSetBaseAttributes class contains the attributes for the curved dimensions.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0f753e4c-b5ed-87df-f138-16e63f6dd96b)

### Constructors
- `public CurvedDimensionSetBaseAttributes()` — Creates a new curved dimension set base attributes instance which loads standard attributes.
- `public CurvedDimensionSetBaseAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new curved dimension set base attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public abstract bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/da11fa4f-a98d-c770-d0e2-f458bc3b82dc)

#### `public abstract bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the specified file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file. Use only the filename, not the extension.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5597a575-45e2-aa83-6576-390f23af3e48)

### Properties
- `Arrowhead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead attributes of the dimension.
- `Color` (DrawingColors, get/set) — The color of the dimension line.
- `Format` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionFormatAttributes, get/set) — The format attributes of the dimension.
- `Placing` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionPlacingAttributes, get/set) — The placing attributes of the dimension.
- `Text` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTextAttributes, get/set) — The text attributes of the dimension.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — The dimension text background transparency. (transparent/opaque)
- `TrueColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (RGB enabled) of the dimension line.

## CurvedDimensionSetHandler (class)

The CurvedDimensionSetHandler class contains operations for creating curved dimension sets.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/3b6dc735-5bad-9707-8004-f93b4f170e3b)

### Constructors
- `public CurvedDimensionSetHandler()` — Initializes a new instance of the CurvedDimensionSetHandler class

### Methods
#### `public CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonal CreateCurvedDimensionSetOrthogonal(ViewBase View, Point ArcPoint1, Point ArcPoint2, Point ArcPoint3, PointList DimensionPoints, double Distance)`

Creates a curved dimension set with orthogonal reference lines from the specified points and inserts it to the database.

**Parameters:**
- `View` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.ViewBase) — The view to be used.
- `ArcPoint1` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The first arc point to be used.
- `ArcPoint2` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The second arc point to be used.
- `ArcPoint3` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The third arc point to be used.
- `DimensionPoints` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.PointList) — The dimension points to be used.
- `Distance` (System.Double) — The distance to be used.

**Returns:** `CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonal` — The created dimension set or null if failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/af2e21ff-e20c-e3ee-7e1f-4f8ce296d084)

#### `public CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonal CreateCurvedDimensionSetOrthogonal(ViewBase View, Point ArcPoint1, Point ArcPoint2, Point ArcPoint3, PointList DimensionPoints, double Distance, CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonal.CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonalAttributes Attributes)`

Creates a curved dimension set with orthogonal reference lines from the specified points and inserts it to the database.

**Parameters:**
- `View` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.ViewBase) — The view to be used.
- `ArcPoint1` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The first arc point to be used.
- `ArcPoint2` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The second arc point to be used.
- `ArcPoint3` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The third arc point to be used.
- `DimensionPoints` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.PointList) — The dimension points to be used.
- `Distance` (System.Double) — The distance to be used.
- `Attributes` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonal.CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonalAttributes) — The attributes to be used.

**Returns:** `CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonal` — The created dimension set or null if failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9ce6fb41-f2ea-6d9d-7a18-34a2644ba4ed)

#### `public CurvedDimensionSetRadial CreateCurvedDimensionSetRadial(ViewBase View, Point ArcPoint1, Point ArcPoint2, Point ArcPoint3, PointList DimensionPoints, double Distance)`

Creates a curved dimension set from the specified points and inserts it to the database. Uses the default attributes.

**Parameters:**
- `View` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.ViewBase) — The view to be used.
- `ArcPoint1` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The first arc point to be used.

- `ArcPoint3` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The third arc point to be used.
- `DimensionPoints` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.PointList) — The dimension points to be used.
- `Distance` (System.Double) — The distance to be used.

**Returns:** `CurvedDimensionSetRadial` — The created dimension set or null if failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a1fb93d0-4b06-952e-ce28-7e5c314d3a4a)

Creates a curved dimension set from the specified points and inserts it to the database.

- `ArcPoint1` (Point, get) — Gets the first arc point.
- `ArcPoint2` (Point, get) — Gets the second arc point.
- `ArcPoint3` (Point, get) — Gets the third arc point.
- `Attributes` (CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonal.CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonalAttributes, get/set) — The curved dimension set orthogonal attributes.
- `DimensionPoints` (PointList, get) — Gets a list of the dimension points.
- `Distance` (Double, get) — Gets the distance. The distance is measured in paper millimeters.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonal.CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonalAttributes (class)

The CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonalAttributes class contains the attributes for managing all the attributes of the curved dimension with orthogonal reference lines.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/16c36036-7daa-fd1f-f952-c51698a43051)

### Constructors
- `public CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonalAttributes()` — Creates a new curved dimension set orthogonal attributes instance which loads standard attributes.
- `public CurvedDimensionSetOrthogonalAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new curved dimension set orthogonal attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/ab0e47b8-1142-af1e-1c0d-d1588ece7aa1)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string attributeFilename)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `attributeFilename` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/cb80bc08-15ad-ac9a-ceeb-471936a6140e)

### Properties
- `Arrowhead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead attributes of the dimension.
- `Color` (DrawingColors, get/set) — The color of the dimension line.
- `Format` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionFormatAttributes, get/set) — The format attributes of the dimension.
- `Placing` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionPlacingAttributes, get/set) — The placing attributes of the dimension.
- `Text` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTextAttributes, get/set) — The text attributes of the dimension.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — The dimension text background transparency. (transparent/opaque)
- `TrueColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (RGB enabled) of the dimension line.

## CurvedDimensionSetRadial.CurvedDimensionSetRadialAttributes (class)

The CurvedDimensionSetRadialAttributes class contains the attributes for managing all the attributes of the curved dimension with radial reference lines.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/93e176d8-7aa3-89da-3e36-7968e250ef86)

### Constructors
- `public CurvedDimensionSetRadialAttributes()` — Creates a new curved dimension set radial attributes instance which loads standard attributes.
- `public CurvedDimensionSetRadialAttributes(bool SkipLoadingAttributes)` — Creates a new curved dimension set radial attributes instance which loads standard attributes. If it set to true attributes won't be lodaded.
- `public CurvedDimensionSetRadialAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new curved dimension set radial attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9f71819e-59e9-7575-c9ec-e020b97186bc)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string attributeFilename)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `attributeFilename` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/f22edb8d-d2ae-0aee-3a63-1acfb794bd69)

### Properties
- `Arrowhead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead attributes of the dimension.
- `Color` (DrawingColors, get/set) — The color of the dimension line.
- `CurvedDimensionType` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.CurvedDimensionTypes, get/set) — The curved dimension type.
- `DimensionType` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTypes, get/set) — The dimension type.
- `Format` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionFormatAttributes, get/set) — The format attributes of the dimension.
- `Placing` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionPlacingAttributes, get/set) — The placing attributes of the dimension.
- `Text` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTextAttributes, get/set) — The text attributes of the dimension.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — The dimension text background transparency. (transparent/opaque)
- `TrueColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (RGB enabled) of the dimension line.

## CurvedSectionMark (class)

The CurvedSectionMark class defines a drawing object that illustrates a curved section in a specific view. In contrast to straight section mark, the curved section mark can only be inserted as a result of creating a curved section view.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d2f96be0-9634-5832-1ef0-03cd0f8aa8dd)

- `RightPoint` (Point, get) — The starting point of the right symbol of the section mark.

## CustomLineType (class)

The CustomLineType class defines the custom line types of the drawing objects.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5029cf06-1d52-5391-2f65-1760cf3721bf)

### Methods
#### `public override Object Clone()`

Creates a new object that is a copy of the current instance.

**Returns:** `Object` — A new object that is a copy of the current instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/03a890bb-7796-54c5-907f-51890915a07a)

#### `public override bool Equals(Object obj)`

Returns true if the current object and the given object are equal.

**Parameters:**
- `obj` (System.Object) — The object we wish to check the equality with.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the given object equals the current object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d0b0011c-d08d-40ac-fdfc-1c8ccd83d064)

#### `public override int GetHashCode()`

Returns a hash code for the object.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The hash code for the object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/60bfd567-f4d8-1eb5-eece-d8d9e16be107)

#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/508fe885-1e18-ed11-81c5-ea7963144e85)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Returns the LineTypes information as a string.

**Returns:** `String` — The LineTypes information as a string.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/33edfbad-ab7c-406a-fec4-5e932e3ea8aa)

### Properties
- `Description` (String, get) — The Description of the custom line type (ASCII representation of the line).
- `LineDescription` (List<Double>, get) — The description of the custom line type. Description uses the following format: List of line segments. Positive value gives length of filled line segment and negative value gives length of empty line segment. 0 means a dot.
- `Name` (String, get) — The Name of the custom line type.

## CustomLineTypeCatalog (class)

## DPMPrinterAttributes (class)

The DPMPrinterAttributes class contains the attributes for controlling the printing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/898f2ba9-b17f-44cf-92b7-08949a658c8e)

### Constructors
- `public DPMPrinterAttributes()` — Creates a new PrintAttributes instance.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0474757e-8df3-02dd-72e3-de832c6ada64)

### Properties
- `ColorMode` (DotPrintColor, get/set) — Output color mode.
- `NumberOfCopies` (Int32, get/set) — The number of copies of each drawing to print. Only applicable for printing to a printer.
- `OpenFileWhenFinished` (Boolean, get/set) — Whether to open file when finished.
- `Orientation` (DotPrintOrientationType, get/set) — The print orientation type.
- `OutputFileName` (String, get/set) — Output file name and path. Applicable in PDF printing.
- `OutputType` (DotPrintOutputType, get/set) — The printing output type.
- `PaperSize` (DotPrintPaperSize, get/set) — The paper size name.
- `PrinterName` (String, get/set) — The printer name.
- `PrintToMultipleSheet` (DotPrintToMultipleSheet, get/set) — Whether to print to multiple sheets.
- `ScaleFactor` (Double, get/set) — The print scale factor.
- `ScalingMethod` (DotPrintScalingType, get/set) — The print scaling method.

## DetailMark (class)

The DetailMark class defines a drawing object that illustrates a detail in a specific view.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/23ec1cfa-34c1-4ed5-2313-46518a3b1bed)

### Constructors
- `public DetailMark(View view, Point centerPoint, Point boundaryPoint, Point labelPoint)` — Creates a new detail mark instance with the given positions.
- `public DetailMark(View view, Point centerPoint, Point boundaryPoint, Point labelPoint, DetailMark.DetailMarkAttributes detailMarkAttributes)` — Creates a new detail mark instance with the given positions and attributes.

## DetailMark.DetailMarkAttributes (class)

The DetailMarkAttributes class is the attributes class for detail marks.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/59e6e3c9-3096-fa15-6c87-984312b000dd)

### Constructors
- `public DetailMarkAttributes()` — Creates a new default DetailMarkAttributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public DetailMarkAttributes(string attributesFile)` — Creates a new DetailMarkAttributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/604b5a6c-d054-ecfe-1a32-06b0a8b7be63)

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `attributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/7c3e3b03-d742-f29f-31f8-4f949e810d91)

### Properties
- `BoundaryLineColor` (DrawingColors, get/set) — Obsolete.
- `BoundaryLineType` (LineTypes, get/set) — Obsolete.
- `BoundaryShape` (DetailMark.DetailMarkAttributes.DetailBoundaryShape, get/set) — The detail mark boundary shape.
- `BoundingLine` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the line type.
- `MarkName` (String, get/set) — The detail mark name.
- `MarkSymbolAttributes` (DetailMarkSymbolAttributes, get/set) — The detail mark symbol attributes.
- `MarkSymbolColor` (DrawingColors, get/set) — 
- `TagsAttributes` (DetailMarkTagsAttributes, get/set) — The attributes for detail mark tags.
- `TrueMarkSymbolColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (rgb enabled) of the detail mark symbol.

## DetailMark.DetailMarkAttributes.DetailBoundaryShape (enum)

The boundary shapes of the detail mark.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/980abf7f-e806-2baf-41e7-ed51ce84ad3b)

### Values
- `None` = `1` — No shape.
- `Circular` = `2` — The circular shape.
- `Rectangular` = `3` — The rectangular shape.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (AttributesBase, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes of the current object. Only attributes of the same type as the object are allowed.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## DimensionLink (class)

The DimensionLink class contains methods related to dimension links. A dimension link is a link between two dimension sets.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/6cb5fe2c-46ef-fd0c-94b8-47fafe4473e8)

### Constructors
- `public DimensionLink(StraightDimensionSet Dimension1, StraightDimensionSet Dimension2)` — Creates a new DimensionLink instance.

### Methods
#### `public override bool Delete()`

Unlinks the dimensions in the current drawing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a10d3ec0-91e6-f06e-6295-b82991528039)

## DimensionSetBase (class)

The DimensionSetBase class is the common base class for all the different dimension sets. The dimension set base cannot be instantiated.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes (class)

The DimensionSetBaseAttributes class is the base class for all dimension set attributes. The class contains attributes that are common to all dimension sets.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c618d777-d2a2-5505-c866-dd12e531fa33)

### Constructors
- `public DimensionSetBaseAttributes()` — Creates a new default dimension set base attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public DimensionSetBaseAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new dimension set base attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public abstract bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/da11fa4f-a98d-c770-d0e2-f458bc3b82dc)

#### `public abstract bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the specified file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file. Use only the filename, not the extension.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5597a575-45e2-aa83-6576-390f23af3e48)

### Properties
- `Arrowhead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead attributes of the dimension.
- `Color` (DrawingColors, get/set) — The color of the dimension line.
- `Format` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionFormatAttributes, get/set) — The format attributes of the dimension.
- `Placing` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionPlacingAttributes, get/set) — The placing attributes of the dimension.
- `Text` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTextAttributes, get/set) — The text attributes of the dimension.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — The dimension text background transparency. (transparent/opaque)
- `TrueColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (RGB enabled) of the dimension line.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.CombineFormats (enum)

The combined dimension formats.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/28b0d248-7c2b-9615-1140-d9f538025098)

### Values
- `Off` = `0` — No combining.
- `NumberOfItemsTimesLength` = `1` — The number of items times the dimension length. E.g. 3*60.
- `NumberOfItemsTimesLengthEqualsResult` = `2` — The number of items times the dimension length equals the result. E.g. 3*60=180.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.CombinedDimensionAttributes (class)

The CombinedDimensionAttributes class handles attributes related to the combination of identical parts of dimensions.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1f848894-eb94-d830-b612-4e021e78f6ed)

### Constructors
- `public CombinedDimensionAttributes()` — Creates a new combined dimension attributes instance. The defaults combine a minimum of three identical dimensions to be shown as the number of items times the length of the dimension.
- `public CombinedDimensionAttributes(int minimumNumberToCombine, DimensionSetBaseAttributes.CombineFormats format)` — Creates a new combined dimension attributes instance with the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/08efea1e-c0ac-8c7c-65e6-b5a6448d8347)

### Properties
- `Format` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.CombineFormats, get/set) — The combined dimension format.
- `MinimumNumberToCombine` (Int32, get/set) — The minimum number of dimensions to be combined.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.CurvedDimensionTypes (enum)

The type of the curved dimension.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/39532c2f-a5f7-6501-f040-7d65feaf3235)

### Values
- `Distance` = `0` — The distance of the points is displayed.
- `Angle` = `1` — The angle is displayed.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionExaggerationAttributes (class)

The DimensionExaggerationAttributes class handles attributes related to the exaggeration of dimension lines.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/ed104708-bf83-237c-9c78-d8dac70012af)

### Constructors
- `public DimensionExaggerationAttributes()` — Creates a new dimension exaggeration attributes instance. The default is to have exaggeration disabled.
- `public DimensionExaggerationAttributes(bool ExaggerationEnabled, DimensionSetBaseAttributes.ExaggerationDirections ExaggerationDirection, DimensionSetBaseAttributes.ExaggerationOrigins ExaggerationOrigin, double ExaggerationWidth, double ExaggerationPosition, double ExaggerationHeight)` — Creates a new dimension exaggeration attributes instance with the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/835f5a19-1bf6-17fd-f6e5-d3605e638016)

### Properties
- `ExaggerationDirection` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.ExaggerationDirections, get/set) — The ExaggerationDirection.
- `ExaggerationEnabled` (Boolean, get/set) — Exaggeration is enabled if the value is true.
- `ExaggerationHeight` (Double, get/set) — The ExaggerationHeight.
- `ExaggerationOrigin` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.ExaggerationOrigins, get/set) — The ExaggerationOrigin.
- `ExaggerationPosition` (Double, get/set) — The ExaggerationPosition.
- `ExaggerationWidth` (Double, get/set) — The ExaggerationWidth.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionFormatAttributes (class)

The DimensionFormatAttributes class controls the precision, format and unit of the dimension.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4d479d66-b47c-fc8f-6223-3abe21e7f513)

### Constructors
- `public DimensionFormatAttributes()` — Creates a new dimension format attributes instance. By default the format rounds the dimension value to the nearest whole and shows it with no decimals. The unit is selected automatically.
- `public DimensionFormatAttributes(DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionValuePrecisions precision, DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionValueFormats format, DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionValueUnits unit)` — Creates a new dimension format attributes instance with the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/72852c3f-ae80-ead6-2076-fe9e89d03971)

### Properties
- `Format` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionValueFormats, get/set) — The format of the dimension.
- `Precision` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionValuePrecisions, get/set) — The precision of the dimension.
- `Unit` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionValueUnits, get/set) — Gets or sets the unit of the dimension. Changing this attribute does not have any effect if the dimension measures an angle.
- `UseDigitGrouping` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether digit grouping should be used or not.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionPlacingAttributes (class)

The DimensionPlacingAttributes class controls the placing of the dimension.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0c652346-d920-5590-e777-e1edf097c620)

### Constructors
- `public DimensionPlacingAttributes()` — Creates a new dimension placing attributes instance. The placing is set to the free placing.
- `public DimensionPlacingAttributes(DimensionSetBaseAttributes.Placings placing, PlacingDirectionAttributes direction, PlacingDistanceAttributes distance)` — Creates a new dimension placing attributes instance with the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/986ea414-b9bc-87b6-0ae1-6992b84173e6)

### Properties
- `Direction` (PlacingDirectionAttributes, get/set) — The placing direction.
- `Distance` (PlacingDistanceAttributes, get/set) — The placing distance attributes.
- `Placing` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.Placings, get/set) — The placing type.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTextAttributes (class)

The DimensionTextAttributes class controls the dimension value representation.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/561bf2b6-be3f-4ceb-1ecc-e3423a4d8e4e)

### Constructors
- `public DimensionTextAttributes()` — Creates a new dimension text attributes instance. By default the default font is used, no frame is drawn and the value text is above the dimension line.
- `public DimensionTextAttributes(FontAttributes font, DimensionSetBaseAttributes.FrameTypes frame, DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTextPlacings textPlacing)` — Creates a new dimension text attributes instance with the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/992e4a4e-49c4-07b6-2ff5-f3206535255c)

### Properties
- `Font` (FontAttributes, get/set) — The font attributes.
- `Frame` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.FrameTypes, get/set) — The frame type of the dimension value text.
- `TextPlacing` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTextPlacings, get/set) — The place of the dimension value text.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTextPlacings (enum)

The placing types of the dimension value text.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/f1b82bd1-7225-0d37-9278-34fa42dd0fef)

### Values
- `AboveDimensionLine` = `0` — The dimension value text is placed above the dimension line.
- `OnDimensionLine` = `1` — The dimension value text is placed on the dimension line.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTypes (enum)

The dimension types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/e83fe43a-91d6-39aa-cd37-17afd7d57023)

### Values
- `Relative` = `1` — The point to point dimensions.
- `Absolute` = `2` — The dimensions from a common starting point.
- `RelativeAndAbsolute` = `3` — The combination of point to point and a common start point.
- `USAbsolute` = `4` — The dimensions from a common starting point, which includes a running dimension mark (RD).
- `USAbsolute2` = `16` — The dimensions from a common starting point, which includes a running dimension mark (RD), but short dimensions are changed to point to point dimensions.
- `AbsoluteWithShortRelatives` = `35` — The dimensions from a common starting point, but short dimensions are changed to point to point dimensions.
- `AbsoluteWithAllRelativesAbove` = `99` — The combination of point to point and a common start point, but the point to point dimensions are placed above the absolute.
- `Elevation` = `8` — Creates an elevation dimension at a picked point.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionValueFormats (enum)

The dimension value formats.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/45e98101-6ab6-c31e-1fc3-97f9cdc23128)

### Values
- `NoDecimals` = `0` — The value is with no decimals.
- `OneOptionalDecimal` = `1` — The value is with one decimal if the value is not whole.
- `OneDecimal` = `2` — The value is with one decimal.
- `TwoOptionalDecimals` = `3` — The value is with two decimals if the value is not whole.
- `TwoDecimals` = `4` — The value is with two decimals.
- `ThreeOptionalDecimals` = `5` — The value is with three decimals if the value is not whole.
- `ThreeDecimals` = `6` — The value is with three decimals.
- `RationalPart` = `7` — The value is with a rational part.
- `SuperscriptEnding` = `8` — The value is with superscript ending.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionValuePrecisions (enum)

The value precisions for the dimensions.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/f40fd295-4398-7fdc-849b-41a1f75b545d)

### Values
- `Whole` = `1` — The value is rounded to the nearest whole (1).
- `OnePerTwo` = `2` — The value is rounded to the nearest half (1/2).
- `OnePerThree` = `3` — The value is rounded to the nearest third (1/3).
- `OnePerFour` = `4` — The value is rounded to the nearest fourth (1/4).
- `OnePerEight` = `8` — The value is rounded to the nearest eighth (1/8).
- `OnePerSixteen` = `16` — The value is rounded to the nearest sixteenth (1/16).
- `OnePerThirtytwo` = `32` — The value is rounded to the nearest thirtysecond (1/32).
- `OnePerTen` = `10` — The value is rounded to one decimal (1/10).
- `OnePerHundred` = `100` — The value is rounded to two decimals (1/100).
- `OnePerThousand` = `1000` — The value is rounded to three decimals (1/1000).
- `TwoAndHalfPerOne` = `-25` — Value is rounded to nearest 2.5mm
- `FivePerOne` = `-50` — Value is rounded to nearest 5mm
- `TenPerOne` = `-100` — Value is rounded to nearest 10mm

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionValueUnits (enum)

The units usable with the dimensions.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5a830ac6-5c02-ae18-226d-39080e9b07f0)

### Values
- `Automatic` = `0` — The unit of the value is selected automatically.
- `Millimeter` = `1` — The value is shown in millimeters.
- `Centimeter` = `2` — The value is shown in centimeters.
- `Meter` = `3` — The value is shown in meters.
- `Inch` = `4` — The value is shown in inches.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.ExaggerationDirections (enum)

The direction of the exaggeration.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5e0ede78-e2b7-43c8-7d69-c7f8b7f8d112)

### Values
- `LeftDown` = `1` — Left / Down.
- `RightUp` = `2` — Right / Up.
- `Both` = `3` — Both Left/Down and Right / Up.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.ExaggerationOrigins (enum)

The origin of the exaggeration.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/bd6376a4-a1fa-6119-c7fd-d82be4ed70c7)

### Values
- `NearDimensionLine` = `1` — Start the exaggeration close to the dimension line.
- `NearMeasuredObject` = `2` — Start the exaggeration as soon as possible as seen from the measured part.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.ExtensionLineTypes (enum)

The extension line types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/8afb849b-02f7-669f-c75e-ae42040fd4cf)

### Values
- `No` = `0` — The extension lines are visible.
- `Yes` = `1` — The extension lines of a dimension are not visible.
- `NotOnGridlines` = `2` — The extension lines are not visible when the lines are on a grid.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.FrameTypes (enum)

The frame types around the dimension values.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/64bbf0bb-5fe1-97d6-6bd5-11eb46ce9a07)

### Values
- `None` = `0` — No frame.
- `Rectangle` = `1` — The frame is a rectangle.
- `Underline` = `2` — The frame is an underline.
- `RoundedRectangle` = `3` — The frame is a rectangle with rounded ends.
- `SharpenedRectangle` = `8` — The frame is a rectangle with sharpened ends.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.Placings (enum)

The placing types of the objects.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/666145f2-0e7a-4353-fff7-0b2204d43a2b)

### Values
- `Free` = `0` — The placing of the object is free.
- `Fixed` = `1` — The placing of the object is fixed.

## DimensionSetBaseAttributes.ShortDimensionTypes (enum)

The position of the short dimension value.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/270e753d-29d1-436c-87f3-b61c61e63d66)

### Values
- `Inside` = `0` — The dimension value is set between the dimension lines when the dimension is too short.
- `Outside` = `1` — The dimension value is moved outside of the dimension when the dimension is too short.

## DotPrintAreaType (enum)

The print area type.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/2b25de02-ff60-ace9-bb0e-0ad759056350)

### Values
- `EntireDrawing` = `0` — Print entire drawing.
- `VisibleArea` = `1` — Print visible area.

## DotPrintColor (enum)

Output color mode.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/edd92030-a090-d4ab-ee00-61bcb093e92b)

- `Color` = `0` — Color mode.

The print orientation type.

- `Auto` = `0` — Auto orientation.

The printer output type.

- `Printer` = `0` — Printer output type.

## DotPrintPaperSize (enum)

The paper size.

- `Auto` = `0` — Printer output type.

## DotPrintScalingType (enum)

The print scaling type.

The print to multiple sheet type.

### Values

- `LeftToRightAndTopToBottom` = `1` — Left to right and top to bottom.
