- `Attributes` (GridLine.GridLineAttributes, get/set) — The attributes of the grid line. For more information see GridLine.GridLineAttributes.
- `EndLabel` (GridLine.GridLabel, get/set) — The end of the grid line, contains the grid label and position information. For more information see GridLine.GridLabel.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `StartLabel` (GridLine.GridLabel, get/set) — The beginning of the grid line, contains the grid label and position information. For more information see GridLine.GridLabel.

## GridLine.GridLabel (class)

The GridLabel class contains the grid labels of the grid lines, the text label and the insertion points.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/dc9cc3a9-7a1e-3320-de0f-a3cd60aad2d5)

### Constructors
- `public GridLabel()` — Creates a new default grid label instance.

### Methods
#### `public RectangleBoundingBox GetAxisAlignedBoundingBox()`

The bounding box of the grid line label (rectangle format, axis aligned).

**Returns:** `RectangleBoundingBox` — The bounding box of the grid line label.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1d86e9d9-3f79-b151-6763-2e07f536c676)

#### `public RectangleBoundingBox GetAxisAlignedBoundingBoxOfTextLabel()`

The bounding box of the grid line label's text (rectangle format, axis aligned).

**Returns:** `RectangleBoundingBox` — The bounding box of the grid line label's text.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/398b10cf-8189-74dc-700f-6bbdc6064484)

#### `public bool MoveObjectRelative(Vector MoveVector)`

Moves the object using the move vector.

**Parameters:**
- `MoveVector` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Vector) — The move vector to be used.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/44eb35f7-0343-00cd-c859-1b58302232d6)

### Properties
- `CenterPoint` (Point, get) — The center point of the grid label.
- `FrameHeight` (Double, get) — The height of the grid label frame.
- `FrameWidth` (Double, get) — The width of the grid label frame.
- `GridLabelPoint` (Point, get/set) — The grid line's label point. If the value of GridLabelPoint differs from the OffsetGridPoint value, a leader line will be created to the GridLabelPoint position. You can remove the leader line by setting GridLabelPoint to the GridPoint value (or by setting the GridLabelPoint anywhere on the grid line, but then the offset will change).
- `GridLabelText` (String, get) — The grid line's label text.
- `GridPoint` (Point, get) — The grid line point, without offsets taken into consideration (model grid line coordinates).
- `OffsetGridPoint` (Point, get) — The grid line point where the grid line actually ends, with offsets applied.
- `TextHeight` (Double, get) — The height of the grid label's text frame.
- `TextWidth` (Double, get) — The width of the grid label's text frame.

## GridLine.GridLineAttributes (class)

The GridLineAttributes class is the attributes class for the grid line.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/00d87eb4-0b57-590a-3618-375be64d212d)

### Constructors
- `public GridLineAttributes()` — Creates a new default grid line attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public GridLineAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new grid line attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/31a350a0-9c84-12c6-74fa-9c2394fbc007)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/056276ed-908e-fa37-4d76-528c0bcd4a9f)

### Properties
- `CustomPresentation` (String, get/set) — Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the object type
- `DrawOnlyTextLabelsNotGridLines` (Boolean, get/set) — Set as true if you don't want to draw the grid lines, only show the grid lines' text labels.
- `DrawTextAtEndOfGridLine` (Boolean, get/set) — Set as true if you want to show the grid line's text label at the end of the line.
- `DrawTextAtStartOfGridLine` (Boolean, get/set) — Set as true if you want to show the grid line's text label at the start of the line.
- `Font` (FontAttributes, get/set) — The grid line's font attributes.
- `Frame` (Frame, get/set) — The frame attributes of the grid line's label.
- `Line` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The grid line's line attributes.
- `OffsetAtEndOfLine` (Double, get/set) — The offset for the end of the grid line.
- `OffsetAtStartOfLine` (Double, get/set) — The offset for the beginning of the grid line.

## HatchAttributes (class)

The HatchAttributes base class, inherited by ModelObjectHatchAttributes and GraphicObjectHatchAttributes, contains hatch attributes.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/f9241cbb-e95d-32d9-1575-c794bf55d860)

### Methods
#### `public abstract bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1fc87d83-33b8-7e4e-7803-dda26a0aa35a)

### Properties
- `Angle` (Double, get/set) — The angle in which to draw the hatch.
- `BackgroundColor` (DrawingHatchColors, get/set) — The background color of the hatch.
- `Color` (DrawingHatchColors, get/set) — The color of the hatch.
- `DrawBackgroundColor` (Boolean, get/set) — Sets whether the background color will be enabled or disabled for the hatch. See also: BackgroundColor.
- `FactorType` (Int32, get/set) — The type of the hatch factor.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the hatch. If an empty string is given as hatch name it will become "None".
- `ScaleX` (Double, get/set) — The scale value of the hatch in the X-direction.
- `ScaleY` (Double, get/set) — The scale value of the hatch in the Y-direction.
- `TrueBackgroundColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (rgb enabled) of the hatch background.
- `TrueColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (RGB enabled) of the hatch.

## Hideable (class)

Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/adf9e63b-0e5b-ebab-0181-098602515f7d)

### Constructors
- `public Hideable()` — Initializes a new instance of the Hideable class

### Methods
#### `public void HideFromDrawing()`

Hides this object from the whole drawing. Calling Modify on the object is required to hide the object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/7feb0562-a85c-0504-cc94-ac7c4da2a769)

#### `public void HideFromDrawingView()`

Hides this object from the drawing view. Calling Modify on the object is required to hide the object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/8bf4d843-e050-fc65-78c4-7e4eb98b11f3)

#### `public void ShowInDrawing()`

Shows this object in the whole drawing. Calling Modify on the object is required to show the object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/ad4c979a-4777-72b6-bff0-f9f01d86bfc2)

#### `public void ShowInDrawingView()`

Shows this object in the drawing view. Calling Modify on the object is required to show the object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/155059b7-6252-a8db-5e4c-7324b6442530)

### Properties
- `IsHidden` (Boolean, get) — Retrieves the information if this object is hidden or not.

## Hideable.HiddenFlags (enum)

The flag for keeping track of objects hidden status.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/aebce6d6-f892-1132-4837-b50f34eb2fe5)

### Values
- `NotHidden` = `0` — Not hidden.
- `HiddenBySelf` = `1` — Hidden by self (meaning nothing else is hiding it).
- `HiddenByParent` = `2` — Hidden by parent (the objects parent is hidden and thus this object is also hidden).

## Hideable.ShouldBeHiddenFlags (enum)

The flag for keeping track if object should be hidden.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b2667dac-9ffe-f073-4d30-08a3983132fd)

### Values
- `ShouldNotBeHidden` = `0` — Should not be hidden, default value.
- `HideFromDrawingView` = `1` — To be hidden from drawing view.
- `HideFromDrawing` = `2` — To be hidden from drawing.
- `ShowInDrawingView` = `3` — To be shown in drawing view.
- `ShowInDrawing` = `4` — To be shown in drawing.

## HyperLink (class)

The HyperLink class defines a hyperlink object in a drawing. When a hyperlink is double clicked, the default browser is launched.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/58821f09-5e89-e43d-574c-0277abbafa24)

### Constructors
- `public HyperLink(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, Uri Target, string Text)` — Creates a new hyperlink instance using standard attributes.
- `public HyperLink(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, Uri Target, string Text, LinkAttributes Attributes)` — Creates a new hyperlink instance using the given attributes.
- `public HyperLink(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, Uri Target, string Text, Size Size)` — Creates a new hyperlink instance using standard attributes.
- `public HyperLink(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, Uri Target, string Text, Size Size, LinkAttributes Attributes)` — Creates a new hyperlink instance using the given attributes.

Deletes the link.

- `Attributes` (LinkAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the link attributes.
- `InsertionPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the insertion point of the link.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `Target` (Uri, get/set) — Gets or sets the target.
- `Text` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the link text that is visible on the screen.

## IAxisAlignedBoundingBox (interface)

The IAxisAlignedBoundingBox interface is implemented by objects that have an axis aligned bounding box.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/be873f02-ea02-6703-2a50-35389c9b66e4)

### Methods
#### `RectangleBoundingBox GetAxisAlignedBoundingBox()`

Gets the bounding box for the current object in the global axis coordinate system.

**Returns:** `RectangleBoundingBox` — A rectangle bounding box for the current object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/f330d78c-7cd8-6e65-9af5-3ffae3df133a)

## IEvents (interface)

Interface for the Events class allows the user to register event listeners for Drawings events. This interface allows the Marshaling of the Events class with Trimble.Remoting

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9b0213b1-c8bf-7f15-b67f-6cadf5155f2a)

### Methods
#### `void Register()`

Registers the instance to listen to the specified events.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a29c697c-fd29-90b1-22a1-186419feb09e)

#### `void UnRegister()`

Unregisters the instance from listening to events.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/33683b4f-4add-0b6d-6743-3adb3e5088ef)

### Events
#### `DrawingChanged` (`Tekla.Structures.Drawing.Events.DrawingStatusChangedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.DrawingStatusChangedDelegate DrawingChanged`

The DrawingChanged event is raised when the drawing has been changed and the database is committed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/23af96ae-5aa6-bcc0-fbcf-a9aa902e36b1)

#### `DrawingDeleted` (`Tekla.Structures.Drawing.Events.DrawingDeletedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.DrawingDeletedDelegate DrawingDeleted`

The DrawingDeleted event is raised when a drawing has been deleted.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/ea943c62-cf02-34f0-bbb3-233020f2c45f)

#### `DrawingInserted` (`Tekla.Structures.Drawing.Events.DrawingInsertedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.DrawingInsertedDelegate DrawingInserted`

The DrawingInserted event is raised when a drawing has been inserted.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/54d4e38c-ded8-dacc-103a-88c89f396649)

#### `DrawingReadyForIssuingChange` (`Tekla.Structures.Drawing.Events.DrawingReadyForIssuingChangeDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.DrawingReadyForIssuingChangeDelegate DrawingReadyForIssuingChange`

The DrawingReadyForIssuingChange event is raised just after a drawing is marked or unmarked ready for issuing.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c543e1bb-632f-9a9f-ee65-56c2aad7163d)

#### `DrawingStatusChanged` (`Tekla.Structures.Drawing.Events.DrawingStatusChangedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.DrawingStatusChangedDelegate DrawingStatusChanged`

The DrawingStatusChanged event is raised when the drawing status has changed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/df0a1a2f-1cd2-f22f-077b-573f4fe8992a)

#### `DrawingUpdated` (`Tekla.Structures.Drawing.Events.DrawingUpdatedDelegate`)

**Signature:** `event Events.DrawingUpdatedDelegate DrawingUpdated`

The DrawingUpdated event is raised when the drawing has been inserted, deleted or modified, and the database is committed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9dce710b-0dca-4fca-7026-3d03cea42217)

## IHasChildren (interface)

The IHasChildren interface is implemented by objects that have child objects.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c8afa60b-3959-4d13-a047-c2a2b6450171)

#### `DrawingObjectEnumerator GetObjects()`

Gets the children of the current object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/e548034d-415e-3fc3-844f-780bc5d2e7c2)

#### `DrawingObjectEnumerator GetObjects(Type[] TypeFilter)`

Gets the children of the current object that are of certain types.

**Parameters:**
- `TypeFilter` (.System.Type.) — The types of the objects to get.

**Returns:** `DrawingObjectEnumerator` — The children of the current object that are of certain types.

## IHideable (interface)

The IHideable interface is used for each object that can be hidden in the drawing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0c4dfdaf-aa13-bb97-dc11-6f890f917d57)

### Properties
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.

## IIsEqual (interface)

The IIsEqual interface is for comparing objects using the function IsEqual().

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5269446d-386e-3171-79e1-c70d903bdb9d)

### Methods
#### `bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with the given object. Checks every field of both objects for equality.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — An object of the same type to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the field values of both objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/f3ea1fd0-1de5-e6c6-e153-fd78b0c22e96)

## IMovableRelative (interface)

The IMovableRelative interface is implemented by objects that can be moved.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/dd7c9522-fae4-f292-0602-24131a70b77e)

### Methods
#### `bool MoveObjectRelative(Vector MoveVector)`

Moves the current object using the move vector. The move vector can be calculated as in the following example.

**Parameters:**
- `MoveVector` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Vector) — The move vector to be used for moving the object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/36bb86bc-dd7e-cfd1-9918-1d6962524d59)

## IObjectAlignedBoundingBox (interface)

The IObjectAlignedBoundingBox interface is implemented by objects that have an object aligned bounding box.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c7cc1640-1aad-5955-defb-1e12e0708cac)

### Methods
#### `RectangleBoundingBox GetObjectAlignedBoundingBox()`

Gets the bounding box for the current object in its local coordinate system.

**Returns:** `RectangleBoundingBox` — A rectangle bounding box for the current object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/06142c5d-cfd3-40c9-493e-9f860a626281)

## IPlacing (interface)

The IPlacing interface is for objects that can have different placings.

## IPreferredPlacing (interface)

The IPreferredPlacing interface is for objects that can have different preferred placing types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/31880a9b-0bd9-17f0-de16-102b1edabb0a)

### Properties
- `PreferredPlacing` (PreferredPlacingTypeBase, get/set) — The type of placing the object should use. See PreferredPlacingTypes for different types of placing.

## Image (class)

The Image class defines an image that can be inserted into a drawing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/362655f7-c664-d22f-3246-90d4d910cdaa)

### Constructors
- `public Image(ViewBase view, Point insertionPoint, string fileName)` — Initializes a new instance of the Image class with the given parameters.
- `public Image(ViewBase view, Point insertionPoint, string fileName, EmbeddedObjectAttributes attributes)` — Initializes a new instance of the Image class using the specified attributes.
- `public Image(ViewBase view, Point insertionPoint, Size frameSize, string fileName)` — Initializes a new instance of the Image class with the given parameters.
- `public Image(ViewBase view, Point insertionPoint, Size frameSize, string fileName, EmbeddedObjectAttributes attributes)` — Initializes a new instance of the Image class using the specified attributes.

Deletes the embedded object.

Changes the string presentation of the drawing object.

### Properties
- `Angle` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle (in degrees).
- `Attributes` (EmbeddedObjectAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes.
- `FileName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the file name defining the embedded object.
- `InsertionPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the insertion point.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `Size` (Size, get/set) — Gets or sets the size of the frame of the embedded object.

## IncludeRevisionMarkEnum (enum)

Options for including a revision info in file name

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/bc567ae8-40ba-d12f-6a83-d4f85959ccd7)

### Values
- `Never` = `0` — Never include revision info
- `Always` = `1` — Always include revision info
- `ByFormatString` = `2` — Include revision info if it's present in the format string, otherwise not

## InsidePartAlongPartOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType (class)

The InsidePartAlongPartOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType class defines a placing type that places the object using the base line placing as the first option. If there is no room for the first option the fallback option will be used. The fallback option is placing the object with a leader line. The base line used is in the middle of the part, aligned with the part.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/2f6e6f1d-f5c8-a4a5-3af3-804fb15825b2)

### Constructors
- `public InsidePartAlongPartOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType()` — Creates a new inside part along part or with leader line placing type instance.

## InsidePartAlongPartPlacingType (class)

The InsidePartAlongPartPlacingType class defines a placing type that places the object using the base line placing. The base line used is in the middle of the part, aligned with the part.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a372b6c0-8b52-bfb8-a605-da56b1d601e8)

### Constructors
- `public InsidePartAlongPartPlacingType()` — Creates a new inside part along part placing type instance.

## InsidePartHorizontalOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType (class)

The InsidePartHorizontalOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType class defines a placing type that places the object using the base line placing as the first option. If there is no room for the first option the fallback option will be used. The fallback option is placing the object with a leader line. The base line used is in the middle of the part, aligned with the part. In addition the object will be rotated to be horizontal in the drawing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0746e3cd-c2cf-6fef-b019-e19d35518c7f)

### Constructors
- `public InsidePartHorizontalOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType()` — Creates a new inside part horizontal or with leader line placing type instance.

## InsidePartHorizontalPlacingType (class)

The InsidePartHorizontalPlacingType class defines a placing type that places the object using the base line placing. The base line used is in the middle of the part, aligned with the part. In addition the object will be rotated to be horizontal in the drawing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/99cc2760-105a-bb05-e486-17b846a7d172)

### Constructors
- `public InsidePartHorizontalPlacingType()` — Creates a new inside part horizontal placing type instance.

## IntList (class)

The IntList class defines a type safe list of integers.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/2f040240-80e1-d7ed-7bc9-e99324180ca9)

### Constructors
- `public IntList()` — Initializes a new instance of the IntList class

### Methods
#### `public int Add(int value)`

Adds a new integer to the end of the string list.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (System.Int32) — The integer to be added to the list.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index where the integer was added.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/047ae331-ab3f-f133-9ad9-44c9e916afbd)

#### `public bool Contains(int value)`

Checks if the given integer is in the list.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (System.Int32) — The integer to be checked.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the integer is in the list, otherwise false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a497d712-0f41-327b-f730-0028d83be6c8)

#### `public IntList GetRange(int index, int count)`

Gets a sub-list of the integer list.

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The start index of the new list.
- `count` (System.Int32) — The number of items in the new list.

**Returns:** `IntList` — The new integer list.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/06faae13-73e4-b5ca-3f59-f42c12381516)

#### `public int IndexOf(int value)`

Gets the index of the specified integer.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (System.Int32) — The integer to locate in the list.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index of the integer if found in the list, otherwise -1.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/2cccec74-0b25-169f-ddbd-11b9360323ad)

#### `public int IndexOf(int value, int startIndex)`

Gets the index of the specified integer between the index to start the search from and the end of the list.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (System.Int32) — The integer to locate in the list.
- `startIndex` (System.Int32) — The index to start the search from.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index of the integer if found in the list, otherwise -1.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/925dae1d-c39e-7149-1d58-f8bcac32e8be)

#### `public int IndexOf(int value, int startIndex, int count)`

Gets the index of the specified integer between the index to start the search from and startIndex + count.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (System.Int32) — The integer to locate in the list.
- `startIndex` (System.Int32) — The index to start the search from.
- `count` (System.Int32) — The number of items searched.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index of the integer if found in the list, otherwise -1.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4ffca6bf-7601-19f3-5313-fc8749dfbdf9)

#### `public void Insert(int index, int value)`

Inserts the integer to the specified index.

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The index where the integer is inserted.
- `value` (System.Int32) — The integer to be inserted.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c256012d-a9b7-0165-7a1c-f7a2c104147c)

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/426ae003-7107-e1f8-06f1-8c6e8dd833ee)

#### `public int LastIndexOf(int value, int startIndex, int count)`

Searches for the last occurence of the integer in the list.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (System.Int32) — The integer to locate.
- `startIndex` (System.Int32) — The index to start the search from.
- `count` (System.Int32) — The number of items searched.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The last index of the integer if found, otherwise -1.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9fa1a7a6-6810-cb91-91eb-27c93769b7d1)

#### `public void Remove(int value)`

Removes the first occurence of the integer from the list.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (System.Int32) — The integer to be removed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/cd8e0284-12af-e850-bf04-e00168f6fe16)

#### `public void RemoveRange(int index, int count)`

Removes a range of items starting from the given index.

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The start index.
- `count` (System.Int32) — The number of items to be removed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/29b15dbf-aae0-4823-6696-87243ba20ad3)

Copies the integers to an integer array.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/8abbc579-908a-e6d0-c687-133300d69e76)

### Properties
- `Item` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the integer at the specified index.

## InvalidAttributesForOperationException (class)

The InvalidAttributesForOperationException class defines the exception that is thrown when an object's attributes are set to something that is not valid for the object.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/13384446-c2d4-0dc5-5a3e-e88fe4fcfe8a)

### Constructors
- `public InvalidAttributesForOperationException(string Message)` — Creates a new exception instance.

## InvalidPluginPickerInputException (class)

The InvalidPluginPickerInputException class defines the exception that is thrown when UI.Picker is used during a Plugin.Insert call but the previously assigned PluginPickerInput does not correspond to the next UI.Picker call.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/502ebf7f-f4ca-c868-7642-373b66416eb9)

### Constructors
- `public InvalidPluginPickerInputException()` — Creates a new InvalidPluginPickerInputException instance.

## InvalidTypeException (class)

The InvalidTypeException class defines the exception that is thrown when a type was not one of the allowed ones.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/6bf4fcbe-a96d-9809-629d-b9491f452c57)

### Constructors
- `public InvalidTypeException(string Message)` — Creates a new InvalidTypeException instance.

## LayoutAttributes (class)

The LayoutAttributes class contains attributes for controlling the drawing layout.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1850def0-b58c-df22-0db5-e61ee3c34036)

### Constructors
- `[ObsoleteAttribute("This constructor is obsolete and may be removed in the future. Instead, use the constructor which takes a Drawing Type.", false)] public LayoutAttributes()` — Creates a new layout attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `[ObsoleteAttribute("This constructor is obsolete and may be removed in the future. Instead, use the constructor which takes a Drawing Type.", false)] public LayoutAttributes(string AttributeFile)` — Creates a new layout attributes instance that loads drawing layout attributes from the specified attribute file.
- `public LayoutAttributes(Drawing Drawing)` — Creates a new layout attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public LayoutAttributes(string AttributeFile, Drawing Drawing)` — Creates a new layout attributes instance that loads drawing layout attributes from the specified attribute file.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/628efe7a-02bf-5d5c-0e53-cf5464e9e70d)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads drawing layout attributes from the specified attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The name of the drawing attribute file. E.g. "standard".

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/be6c4b9e-a9a4-b5c7-e99c-a88c1d33ebcc)

### Properties
- `AutoSizeOptions` (AutoSizeOptions, get/set) — Gets or sets the auto size options that control the drawing sheet size. Setting this property will change the SizeDefinitionMode to AutoSize.
- `ListHiddenObjectsInTemplates` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets the property for controlling whether to list hidden objects in templates.
- `SheetSize` (Size, get/set) — Gets or sets the sheet size. Setting this property will change the SizeDefinitionMode to SpecifiedSize.
- `SizeDefinitionMode` (SizeDefinitionMode, get/set) — Gets or sets the size definition mode.

## LeaderLine (class)

The LeaderLine class defines a leader line attached to a parent object, for example a Text or a Mark. The LeaderLine can be modified to add/remove elbow points or to change where it is pointing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/f7503a7c-8599-f10f-cd3f-d9632596af1e)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (AttributesBase, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes of the current object. Only attributes of the same type as the object are allowed.
- `ElbowPoints` (PointList, get/set) — The leader line's elbow points (middle points).
- `EndPoint` (Point, get) — The end point of the leader line (set by the parent object).
- `LeaderLineType` (LeaderLine.LeaderLineTypes, get) — Gets the type of the leader line. For a list of the available leader line types see LeaderLine.LeaderLineTypes.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — The starting point of the leader line.

## LeaderLine.LeaderLineTypes (enum)

The different types of leader lines available.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/7cf6ff97-74c7-7dab-6c0b-1daf30c10428)

### Values
- `Undefined` = `0` — No type defined yet (this means that the leader line isn't selected yet).
- `NormalLeaderLine` = `1` — The standard leader line type. Its start point and elbow points can be freely modified. The end point is read-only information.
- `SupportLeaderLine` = `2` — The support leader line type, used in reinforcement marks with parallel leader lines. All leader line information is read-only.
- `ExtensionLeaderLine` = `3` — The type for the extension leader lines (enabled by the usage of an advanced option). All leader line information is read-only.

## LeaderLineAndParentObjectAlongPartPlacingType (class)

## LevelMark (class)

The LevelMark class defines a drawing object that is displayed as a level mark in a drawing. See the Tekla Structures help for more information.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/fce74c34-2166-28c3-f4db-792a8ecdff6c)

- `public LevelMark(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, Point BasePoint, LevelMark.LevelMarkAttributes Attributes)` — Initializes a new instance of the LevelMark class using the given attributes.
- `public LevelMark(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, Point BasePoint, ModelObject modelObject)` — Initializes a new instance of the LevelMark class using the given attributes.
- `public LevelMark(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, Point BasePoint, PlacingBase Placing)` — Initializes a new instance of the LevelMark class using the given attributes.
- `public LevelMark(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, Point BasePoint, ModelObject modelObject, LevelMark.LevelMarkAttributes Attributes)` — Initializes a new instance of the LevelMark class using the given attributes.
- `public LevelMark(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, Point BasePoint, PlacingBase Placing, LevelMark.LevelMarkAttributes Attributes)` — Initializes a new instance of the LevelMark class using the given attributes.
- `public LevelMark(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, Point BasePoint, PlacingBase Placing, ModelObject modelObject)` — Initializes a new instance of the LevelMark class using the given attributes.
- `public LevelMark(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, Point BasePoint, PlacingBase Placing, ModelObject modelObject, LevelMark.LevelMarkAttributes Attributes)` — Initializes a new instance of the LevelMark class using the given attributes.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (LevelMark.LevelMarkAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the level mark attributes.
- `BasePoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the level mark base point.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Gets the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `InsertionPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the level mark insertion point.
- `ModelObjectIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — Gets the model object identifier
- `Placing` (PlacingBase, get/set) — Gets or sets the current placing of the object. See the placing types for different placing options.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `SubType` (LevelMark.LevelMarkType, get/set) — Gets or sets the subtype of the level mark.

## LevelMark.LevelMarkAttributes (class)

The LevelMarkAttributes class contains the attributes for the level mark.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/ac4e8119-c21c-dd94-2a5c-f7537c74525d)

### Constructors
- `public LevelMarkAttributes()` — Initializes a new instance of the LevelMark.LevelMarkAttributes class that loads standard attributes.
- `public LevelMarkAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Initializes a new instance of the LevelMark.LevelMarkAttributes class that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/6a5f89ee-75fb-acf8-f90a-70311f0a3c83)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/61650b6f-be32-1dab-3b09-8256f7189601)

### Properties
- `Angle` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle of the level mark, in degrees.
- `ArrowHead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the arrow attributes for the level mark.
- `Font` (FontAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the font of the level mark text.
- `Frame` (Frame, get/set) — Gets or sets the frame attributes for the level mark.
- `PlacingAttributes` (PlacingAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the placing attributes that the object should use. Using these you can specify whether the object is automatically arranged in the drawing or not.
- `Postfix` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets text after the level value.
- `Prefix` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the text before the level value.
- `TextHidden` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the numeric values are visible or hidden.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the transparency of the background of the level mark text is enabled.
- `Unit` (UnitAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the unit attributes.
- `UseGrouping` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether to use different grouping options to represent the level mark dimensions.
- `UsePositiveSignForPositiveLevels` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether a + character in front of positive level value.

## LevelMark.LevelMarkType (enum)

Defines options about how the level mark is shown.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/2f6865a2-2311-b832-fc45-e2d34079958b)

### Values
- `NoArrowNoLeaderLine` = `0` — Level mark is shown without an arrow and a leader line.
- `ArrowWithoutLeaderLine` = `1` — Level mark is shown with an arrow but without a leader line.
- `InclinedLeaderLine` = `2` — Level mark is shown with an inclined leader line but without an arrow.
- `OrthogonalLeaderLine` = `3` — Level mark is shown with an orthogonal leader line and with an arrow.

## Line (class)

The Line class defines a line that is a two-point line with an optional bulge (curve).

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/363f7e93-fecf-3cde-bf2b-d3d7e69612f2)

### Constructors
- `public Line(ViewBase view, Point startPoint, Point endPoint)` — Creates a new line instance with two points and no bulge (straight line) using standard attributes.
- `public Line(ViewBase view, Point startPoint, Point endPoint, double bulge)` — Creates a new line instance with two points and a bulge using standard attributes.
- `public Line(ViewBase view, Point startPoint, Point endPoint, Line.LineAttributes inAttributes)` — Creates a new line instance with two points and no bulge (straight line) using the given attributes.
- `public Line(ViewBase view, Point startPoint, Point endPoint, double bulge, Line.LineAttributes attributes)` — Creates a new line instance with two points and a bulge using the given attributes.

- `Attributes` (Line.LineAttributes, get/set) — The attributes for the line.
- `Bulge` (Double, get/set) — The bulge (curve) to use for the line (a bulge is the ratio between the height and width of an object).
- `EndPoint` (Point, get/set) — The end point of the line.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — The start point of the line.

## LineTypesEnum (enum)

The line types (the presentation of the line).

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/74868e4d-6ded-4dee-92eb-c69c3abb316c)

### Values
- `UndefinedLine` = `0` — The line type is undefined.
- `SolidLine` = `1` — The solid line (default).
- `DashedLine` = `2` — The dashed line (-----).
- `SlashedLine` = `3` — The slashed line (- - -).
- `DashDot` = `4` — The dash dotted line (--.--.).
- `DottedLine` = `5` — The dotted line (.....).
- `DashDoubleDot` = `6` — The dash double dotted line (-..-..-).
- `SlashDash` = `7` — The slash dash line (-- - -- -).
- `Custom` = `9999` — The custom line type (it will also transport the custom line type name)

## LinkAttributes (class)

The LinkAttributes class contains the attributes for controlling the appearance of a link.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4fe70624-5381-eb78-a7a4-17802129c21d)

### Constructors
- `public LinkAttributes()` — Creates a new link attributes instance using standard attributes.
- `public LinkAttributes(string AttributeFile)` — Creates a new link attributes instance loading the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d0248c23-25fb-914d-c803-44c1ad907136)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the link frame attributes from the specified file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The name of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if succeeded.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9aa04884-118a-a851-bc6c-be135647aa0d)

### Properties
- `Font` (FontAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the font attributes.
- `Frame` (LinkFrameAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the link frame attributes.
- `IsUnderlined` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the link is underlined or not.
- `Scaling` (ScalingOptions, get/set) — Gets or sets the link scaling options.

## LinkBase (class)

#### `public abstract bool Insert()`

Inserts the object instance into the database. The inserted instance must be of a real type.

#### `public abstract bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with the given object. Checks every field of both objects for equality.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — An object of the same type to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/ef079c64-f745-35e7-e5c0-4b2c2bc93d02)

Returns true if the current object and the given object are referencing the same database object.

- `Object` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.DatabaseObject) — The other object to be used.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the objects are referencing the same database object.

Resizes the object. The scaling type is changed to ScaleToFit.

### Properties
- `Angle` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle of the link.
- `Attributes` (LinkAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the link attributes.
- `InsertionPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the insertion point of the link.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `Text` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the link text that is visible on the screen.

## Mark (class)

The Mark class represents a mark object that contains a single mark. Several mark objects can be merged into one mark set.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/27fcc7fe-3bc1-d692-2b30-83c4fe213fb1)

### Constructors
- `public Mark(ModelObject modelObject)` — Constructs a Mark that will be attached to the specified ModelObject upon Insert.

### Methods
#### `public override bool Delete()`

Calls the system to delete the mark attached to a model object. After this operation, only associative notes can be attached to that model object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/e6aad9bc-bff0-99b1-7612-de5aaf232658)

- `Value` (System.Double) — The new value for the property.

#### `public override string ToString()`

Changes the string presentation of the drawing object.

**Returns:** `String` — The name of the current drawing object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/23e70841-4b0d-9dc2-7a9b-f3b6d7eed19f)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (Mark.MarkAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the mark attributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `InsertionPoint` (Point, get/set) — The insertion point of the mark.
- `IsAssociativeNote` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the mark is an associative note.
- `Placing` (PlacingBase, get/set) — The current placing of the object. See the placing types for different placing options.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## Mark.MarkAttributes (class)

The MarkAttributes class defines the mark attributes.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4dede4c2-574c-e381-baef-66b0c36c9fdf)

### Constructors
- `public MarkAttributes(ModelObject modelObject)` — Creates a new default mark attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public MarkAttributes(ModelObject modelObject, string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new mark attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/48571435-fb53-ae1c-8991-522c0f153733)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string attributeFilename)`

Loads attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `attributeFilename` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/bc73e5ae-f88b-8971-2354-196cf03b0255)

### Properties
- `Angle` (Double, get) — The actual angle of the mark, in degrees. Can differ from the rotation angle set by the user for marks, e.g. 45-degree column marks in GA drawings or when placing mark along part edges.
- `ArrowHead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead of the leader line(s).
- `Content` (ContainerElement, get/set) — The mark content as a list of elements.
- `CustomPresentation` (String, get/set) — Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the object type
- `Frame` (Frame, get/set) — The frame attributes of the mark's labels.
- `PlacingAttributes` (PlacingAttributes, get/set) — The placing attributes that the object should use. Using these you can specify whether the object is automatically arranged in the drawing or not.
- `PreferredPlacing` (PreferredPlacingTypeBase, get/set) — The type of placing the object should use. See the preferred placing types for different types of placing.
- `RotationAngle` (Double, get/set) — The mark rotation angle set by the user. Unit is degrees.
- `TextAlignment` (MarkBase.MarkBaseAttributes.TextAlignOptions, get/set) — The mark text alignment.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — The mark text background transparency. (transparent/opaque)

## MarkBase (class)

## MarkBase.MarkBaseAttributes (class)

The MarkBaseAttributes class is the attributes class for the mark base.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0a855c11-4d75-8d03-91e3-450ddbd154f4)

### Constructors
- `public MarkBaseAttributes()` — Creates a new default mark base attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public MarkBaseAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new mark base attributes instance with the given attributes file's filename.

### Methods
#### `public abstract bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/da11fa4f-a98d-c770-d0e2-f458bc3b82dc)

#### `public abstract bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the specified file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file. Use only the filename, not the extension.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5597a575-45e2-aa83-6576-390f23af3e48)

### Properties
- `Angle` (Double, get) — The actual angle of the mark, in degrees. Can differ from the rotation angle set by the user for marks, e.g. 45-degree column marks in GA drawings or when placing mark along part edges.
- `ArrowHead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead of the leader line(s).
- `CustomPresentation` (String, get/set) — Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the object type
- `Frame` (Frame, get/set) — The frame attributes of the mark's labels.
- `PlacingAttributes` (PlacingAttributes, get/set) — The placing attributes that the object should use. Using these you can specify whether the object is automatically arranged in the drawing or not.
- `PreferredPlacing` (PreferredPlacingTypeBase, get/set) — The type of placing the object should use. See the preferred placing types for different types of placing.
- `RotationAngle` (Double, get/set) — The mark rotation angle set by the user. Unit is degrees.
- `TextAlignment` (MarkBase.MarkBaseAttributes.TextAlignOptions, get/set) — The mark text alignment.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — The mark text background transparency. (transparent/opaque)

## MarkBase.MarkBaseAttributes.TextAlignOptions (enum)

Enumeration of the possible text alignment values.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b95d051a-03df-2ce9-2ed6-62caf2b0d0df)

### Values
- `Left` = `0` — Text is left-aligned.
- `Center` = `1` — Text is center-aligned.
- `Right` = `2` — Text is right-aligned.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (MarkSet.MarkSetAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the mark set attributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `InsertionPoint` (Point, get/set) — The insertion point of the mark.
- `IsAssociativeNote` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the mark is an associative note.
- `Placing` (PlacingBase, get/set) — The current placing of the object. See the placing types for different placing options.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## MarkSet.MarkSetAttributes (class)

The MarkSetAttributes class is the attribute class for the mark set.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/02a61a8d-5979-873a-a818-588030444c16)

### Constructors
- `public MarkSetAttributes()` — Initializes a new instance of the MarkSet.MarkSetAttributes class

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/52043a5a-0ded-dae1-433e-b78dbf2bd68a)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string attributeFilename)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file. Not implemented yet.

**Parameters:**
- `attributeFilename` (System.String) — The attribute file's filename.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — False always.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/000e97d7-16e6-56b8-9ede-6153fecbc722)

### Properties
- `Angle` (Double, get) — The actual angle of the mark, in degrees. Can differ from the rotation angle set by the user for marks, e.g. 45-degree column marks in GA drawings or when placing mark along part edges.
- `ArrowHead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead of the leader line(s).
- `CustomPresentation` (String, get/set) — Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the object type
- `Frame` (Frame, get/set) — The frame attributes of the mark's labels.
- `PlacingAttributes` (PlacingAttributes, get/set) — The placing attributes that the object should use. Using these you can specify whether the object is automatically arranged in the drawing or not.
- `PreferredPlacing` (PreferredPlacingTypeBase, get/set) — The type of placing the object should use. See the preferred placing types for different types of placing.
- `RotationAngle` (Double, get/set) — The mark rotation angle set by the user. Unit is degrees.
- `TextAlignment` (MarkBase.MarkBaseAttributes.TextAlignOptions, get/set) — The mark text alignment.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — The mark text background transparency. (transparent/opaque)

## MarkSymbolShape (enum)

The mark symbol shapes for the detail mark.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/94c5a91d-8f13-ba70-192b-ea34fcbbc28c)

### Values
- `None` = `1` — No shape.
- `Circular` = `2` — The circular shape.
- `SlashedCircular` = `3` — The slashed circular shape.
- `Custom` = `4` — The custom shape.

## ModelObject (class)

The ModelObject class is an abstract class for all model objects in the drawing side. The model objects of the drawing side contain identifiers of the actual model objects in the model database. The model objects appear and disappear based on current view properties (view size, settings).

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/415b2552-1c37-d8ba-b1bd-59a60b8f0783)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (AttributesBase, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes of the current object. Only attributes of the same type as the object are allowed.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## ModelObjectHatchAttributes (class)

The ModelObjectHatchAttributes class contains hatch attributes for model object hatches.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/50587d82-2278-88b6-491b-0a4c58015d81)

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4d971521-0161-e151-2ecc-52e2351a380c)

### Properties
- `Angle` (Double, get/set) — The angle in which to draw the hatch.
- `AutomaticScaling` (Boolean, get/set) — Specifies whether automatic scaling is enabled. If disabled, custom scale values will be used (see ScaleX and ScaleY).
- `BackgroundColor` (DrawingHatchColors, get/set) — The background color of the hatch.
- `Color` (DrawingHatchColors, get/set) — The color of the hatch.
- `DrawBackgroundColor` (Boolean, get/set) — Sets whether the background color will be enabled or disabled for the hatch. See also: BackgroundColor.
- `FactorType` (Int32, get/set) — The type of the hatch factor.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the hatch. If an empty string is given as hatch name it will become "None".
- `ScaleX` (Double, get/set) — The scale value of the hatch in the X-direction.
- `ScaleY` (Double, get/set) — The scale value of the hatch in the Y-direction.
- `TrueBackgroundColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (rgb enabled) of the hatch background.
- `TrueColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (RGB enabled) of the hatch.

## MultiDrawing (class)

The MultiDrawing class is for handling multidrawings.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/fc3ba5fd-4ff0-8683-dcb2-929e61446a07)

### Methods
#### `public bool CommitChanges()`

Commits the changes made to the drawings database so far. Commit inserts a kind of save-point into the database and the save-point will be retrieved if the user does undo. Commit also executes all messages. Please note that you need to also to save the drawings database to persists the changes to disk.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/bc26a611-bd6a-535a-4461-943b8bf5351c)

#### `public bool CommitChanges(string Message)`

Commits the changes made to the drawings database so far. Commit inserts a kind of save-point into the database and the save-point will be retrieved if the user does undo. Commit also executes all messages. Please note that you need to also to save the drawings database to persists the changes to disk.

**Parameters:**
- `Message` (System.String) — The message to be saved with the commit.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/92109855-5313-7446-bef4-ab6dde6875f1)

**Remarks:** Note: The returned plot file name can contain invalid file name characters.

- `includeRevisionInfo` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.IncludeRevisionMarkEnum) — How or if to include revision info.

**Returns:** `String` — The plot file name or an empty string if an error occured.

### Properties
- `CommitMessage` (String, get) — Gets the commit message
- `CreationDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing creation date.
- `DrawingTypeStr` (String, get) — The type of the drawing
- `IsFrozen` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is frozen.
- `IsIssued` (Boolean, get) — Describes whether the drawing is issued.
- `IsIssuedButModified` (Boolean, get) — Describes whether the drawing is issued but modified since it was issued.
- `IsLocked` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is locked.
- `IsLockedBy` (String, get) — Gets the logged in username that set the drawing to locked
- `IsMasterDrawing` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is a master drawing (shown with a special symbol on the drawing list).
- `IsReadyForIssue` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is ready for issue.
- `IsReadyForIssueBy` (String, get) — Gets the logged in username that set the drawing Ready for issue
- `IssuingDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing issuing date.
- `Layout` (LayoutAttributes, get) — Gets the drawing layout attributes.
- `Mark` (String, get) — The drawing mark.
- `ModificationDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing modification date.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the drawing.
- `OutputDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing output date. XS_DISABLE_DRAWING_PLOT_DATE controls whether this date is set.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `SectionViewStartLabel` (String, get) — Gets the drawing attribute for start label of section view
- `Title1` (String, get/set) — The first drawing title.
- `Title2` (String, get/set) — The second drawing title.
- `Title3` (String, get/set) — The third drawing title.
- `UpToDateStatus` (DrawingUpToDateStatus, get) — Gets the drawing up to date status.

## OpenGraphicObject.OpenGraphicObjectAttributes (class)

- `Arrowhead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead attributes of the open graphic object. The end points of an open graphic object can contain arrowheads.
- `Line` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line attributes of the graphic object. A graphic object always consists of lines.

## Part (class)

The Part class contains methods related to parts. A part is a drawing object derived from a model object. It represents a drawing part that has a reference to the actual part in the model database.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/85faee90-0121-460f-f6fe-65d1f54a33d3)

- `values` (System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<String,Int32>.) — Dictionary of name/value pairs that contain the values of the found user properties with the given names.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the request succeeded (the returned values can still be empty if no such UserProperties were found).

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b485bd81-5ccf-68e4-0453-caea6b1d0dde)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (Part.PartAttributes, get/set) — The attributes of the part. For more information see Part.PartAttributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## Part.PartAttributes (class)

The PartAttributes class is the attributes class for the part.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/08d50d99-aaab-1778-91d0-0deab85b3dff)

### Constructors
- `public PartAttributes()` — Creates a new default part attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public PartAttributes(string AttributeFile)` — Creates a new part attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/471a1278-b484-0fca-5b96-d7755761df7a)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1dfc4ac7-a2b3-bf89-87af-1fc429293cb0)

### Properties
- `CenterLine` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of the center line.
- `CustomPresentation` (String, get/set) — Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the object type
- `DrawCenterLine` (Boolean, get/set) — True if the center line is drawn.
- `DrawChamfers` (Boolean, get/set) — True if chamfers are drawn.
- `DrawConnectingSideMarks` (Boolean, get/set) — True if connecting side marks are drawn.
- `DrawHiddenLines` (Boolean, get/set) — True if hidden lines are drawn.
- `DrawOrientationMark` (Boolean, get/set) — True if the orientation mark is drawn.
- `DrawOwnHiddenLines` (Boolean, get/set) — True if own hidden lines are drawn.
- `DrawPopMarks` (Boolean, get/set) — True if pop marks are drawn.
- `DrawReferenceLine` (Boolean, get/set) — True if the reference line is drawn.
- `FaceHatch` (ModelObjectHatchAttributes, get/set) — The hatch type of the face hatch.
- `HiddenLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of hidden lines.
- `OwnHiddenLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of own hidden lines.
- `PartialProfileLength` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the partial profile length.
- `PartialProfileOffset` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the partial profile offset.
- `ReferenceLine` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of the reference line.
- `Representation` (Part.Representation, get/set) — The representation of the part.
- `SectionFaceHatch` (ModelObjectHatchAttributes, get/set) — The hatch type of the section face hatch.
- `SectionLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of section lines.
- `SymbolOffset` (Double, get/set) — The symbol offset.
- `VisibleLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of visible lines.

## Part.Representation (enum)

The part representations.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/7c7b3c48-8df3-697d-d3d0-a3e06f4c467c)

### Values
- `Outline` = `1` — The outline representation.
- `Symbol` = `2` — The symbol representation.
- `WorkshopForm` = `4` — The workshop form representation.
- `Exact` = `8` — The exact representation.
- `BoundingBox` = `16` — The bounding box representation.
- `BaseBox` = `32` — The base box representation.
- `SolidWithPartialProfile` = `64` — The solid with partial profile representation.

## PickerInputInterrupt (class)

The PickerInputInterrupt class represents the interruption of picking by the user (e.g. by pressing Escape while requested to pick points). Used for values asked by any UI.Picker.Pick* method where an interrupt happens by the user.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9294b17b-788d-fdf8-9238-bbd7d9b0c2d3)

## PickerInputNPoints (class)

The PickerInputNPoints class represents a pick of several points in a view. Used for values asked by UI.Picker.PickPoints(StringList Prompts, out PointList PickedPoints, out ViewBase PickedView) and UI.Picker.PickPoints(int NumberOfPicks, StringList Prompts, out PointList PickedPoints, out ViewBase PickedView).

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/3ffa5a86-d245-1c64-7261-2cff99d38314)

### Constructors
- `public PickerInputNPoints(ViewBase pickedView, PointList pickedPoints)` — Creates a new PickerInputNPoints instance representing N points selected in a view.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0a458ed1-3080-d293-0f63-3e6346bb244b)

### Properties
- `PickedPoints` (PointList, get/set) — Gets or sets the list of picked points in the view coordinates.
- `PickedView` (ViewBase, get/set) — The view within which the pick occurred.

## PickerInputObjectAndAPoint (class)

The PickerInputObjectAndAPoint class represents a pick of an object in a view. It provides both the picked object and the point used to pick the object. Used for values asked by UI.Picker.PickObject(string Prompt, out DrawingObject PickedObject, out ViewBase PickedView, out Point PickedPoint).

- `PickedView` (ViewBase, get/set) — The view within which the pick occurred.

## PlacingAttributes (class)

The PlacingAttributes class contains the basic attributes for determining an object's placing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/22655d18-229c-1261-e042-94b9a0bc6cd7)

### Constructors
- `public PlacingAttributes()` — Creates a new placing attributes instance. The default parameters are: IsFixed = true, default PlacingDistanceAttributes, default PlacingQuarterAttributes.
- `public PlacingAttributes(bool IsFixed, PlacingDistanceAttributes PlacingDistance, PlacingQuarterAttributes PlacingQuarter)` — Creates a new placing quarter attributes instance with the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public Object Clone()`

Creates a new object that is a copy of the current instance.

**Returns:** `Object` — A new object that is a copy of the current instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/8cd456cb-5f60-4c00-94fd-ce8f161a4c33)

#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5788119b-9502-d9c7-a144-ca2179d1b643)

### Properties
- `IsFixed` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the object is using fixed or free placing. If set to true, it means that fixed placing is enabled, and the object will not be rearranged in the drawing automatically. If set to false, then free placing is enabled, and the object will be placed in the most appropriate location near the original location.
- `PlacingDistance` (PlacingDistanceAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the placing distance attributes.
- `PlacingQuarter` (PlacingQuarterAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the placing quarter attributes.

## PlacingBase (class)

The PlacingBase class is the base class for placing types. If you set a user defined Placing to an object it will override the PreferredPlacingType set in the object's attributes. Please note that not all objects accept all types. If you try to use the wrong type it will not be accepted (Insert, Modify will fail). (It is strongly recommended to use the PreferredPlacingTypes to assign new Placings to objects.)

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/cef90e8e-aad5-4ce3-8efe-9d223a56b4e1)

### Methods
#### `public abstract bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/15f0b136-8c99-9174-4b69-90dcd3963d00)

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9505e7d9-339c-5f35-f0bb-e40e256106e9)

### Properties
- `Negative` (Boolean, get/set) — The negative direction of placing.
- `Positive` (Boolean, get/set) — The positive direction of placing.

## PlacingDistanceAttributes (class)

The PlacingDistanceAttributes class contains the basic attributes for placing distances.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/63b1eb62-5a40-d347-0a8a-46e21aa63e49)

### Constructors
- `public PlacingDistanceAttributes()` — Initializes a new instance of the PlacingDistanceAttributes class. The default values are a 0.0 search margin and a 0.0 minimal distance.
- `public PlacingDistanceAttributes(double searchMargin, double minimalDistance)` — Creates a new placing distance attributes instance with the given search marginal and minimal distance.
- `public PlacingDistanceAttributes(double searchMargin, double minimalDistance, double maximalDistance)` — Creates a new placing distance attributes instance with the given search margin, minimal and maximal distance.

### Methods
#### `public Object Clone()`

Creates a new object that is a copy of the current instance.

**Returns:** `Object` — A new object that is a copy of the current instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b6d8d2ac-5201-c26c-4db8-c01199cdcdd4)

#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/f6c990a4-f8d1-b02b-2b83-2f794879511e)

### Properties
- `MaximalDistance` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the maximal distance for placing.
- `MinimalDistance` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the minimal distance for placing.
- `SearchMargin` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the search marginal for placing.

## PlacingQuarterAttributes (class)

The PlacingQuarterAttributes class contains the basic attributes for placing quarters.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c71a59d4-6836-e9f2-a51e-09c64d4ed72d)

### Constructors
- `public PlacingQuarterAttributes()` — Creates a new placing quarter attributes instance. The default parameters are: TopLeft = true, TopRight = true, BottomLeft = true, BottomRight = true.
- `public PlacingQuarterAttributes(bool TopLeft, bool TopRight, bool BottomLeft, bool BottomRight)` — Creates a new placing quarter attributes instance with the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public Object Clone()`

Creates a new object that is a copy of the current instance.

**Returns:** `Object` — A new object that is a copy of the current instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/3528770e-cd45-9205-73d7-b00c3e84a270)

#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c39a275d-9cb2-f970-34d2-f2fcf3beacb2)

### Properties
- `BottomLeft` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the object can search for a suitable location in the bottom left direction.
- `BottomRight` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the object can search for a suitable location in the bottom right direction.
- `TopLeft` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the object can search for a suitable location in the top left direction.
- `TopRight` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the object can search for a suitable location in the top right direction.

## PlacingTypes (class)

The PlacingTypes class defines the different available placing types. Please note that not all objects accept all types. If you try to use the wrong type it will not be accepted (Insert, Modify will fail).

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/38d18df2-bc23-3978-7a9f-cb48e7d91ae3)

### Methods
#### `public static PlacingBase AlongLinePlacing(Point StartPoint, Point EndPoint)`

The object is located and moved along a line defined by two points.

**Parameters:**
- `StartPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The first point of the line.
- `EndPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The second point of the line.

**Returns:** `PlacingBase` — A new along line placing instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0a360cc9-c148-aba4-f799-70c87d9cee9d)

#### `public static PlacingBase BaseLinePlacing(Point StartPoint, Point EndPoint)`

The object is placed using a line as a reference. This will thus define the object's X-axis along that line.

**Parameters:**
- `StartPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The first point of the line.
- `EndPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The second point of the line.

**Returns:** `PlacingBase` — A new base line placing instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/350d8df2-b3ab-8e63-0a45-5e25d7eef0a5)

#### `public static PlacingBase LeaderLinePlacing(Point LeaderLineStartPoint)`

The object is placed attached to a leader line.

**Parameters:**
- `LeaderLineStartPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The start point of the leader line.

**Returns:** `PlacingBase` — A new leader line placing instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/db02754b-9b64-ac06-1603-6ed790b6d33f)

#### `public static PlacingBase PointPlacing()`

The object is placed at one point (the insertion point) without any additional placing options.

**Returns:** `PlacingBase` — A new point placing instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a01a7987-8b62-391d-46ce-1746577b2c25)

## Plugin (class)

The Plugin class represents a drawing plug-in.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4b3cdf4c-0872-f97b-d56e-5209179f1fca)

### Constructors
- `public Plugin(ViewBase View, string Name)` — Creates a new plug-in instance.

### Methods
#### `public bool ApplyStandardValues(string Filename)`

Applies the values of the specified file as the default values for the dialog belonging to this plug-in. This will affect each Insert call of the plug-ins with the same name.

**Parameters:**
- `Filename` (System.String) — The short name of the file containing the attributes.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success. Otherwise, false.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/6c51b9d0-7066-d66d-27cd-9d428d3d1d72)

#### `public override bool Delete()`

Deletes the plug-in from the current drawing database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/84960371-6f53-b251-560c-d1a016ff3245)

#### `public double GetDoubleAttribute(string FieldName)`

Gets the value of the specified double attribute of the plug-in.

**Parameters:**
- `FieldName` (System.String) — The name of the attribute to get.

**Returns:** `Double` — The value of the attribute, or 0 if the plug-in does not have such an attribute.

Retrieves all double user properties for the object with the given list of names.

**Parameters:**
- `values` (System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<String,Double>.) — Dictionary of name/value pairs that contain the values of the found user properties with the given names.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the request succeeded (the returned values can still be empty if no UserProperties were found).

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b25f0e6b-ab5c-154a-a45c-4f5868a20599)

- `values` (System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<String,Double>.) — Dictionary of name/value pairs that contain the values of the found user properties with the given names.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the request succeeded (the returned values can still be empty if no such UserProperties were found).
