### Properties
- `Attributes` (AttributesBase, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes of the current object. Only attributes of the same type as the object are allowed.
- `Name` (String, get) — Gets the name of the plug-in.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## PluginPickerInput (class)

The PluginPickerInput class represents a predefined set of picker inputs that can be provided to a plug-in object. It replaces the interactive selection of points and/or objects by the user when the plug-in is inserted into a drawing through the API.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1fbe56ff-0633-90a5-ef51-20307ee497cb)

### Constructors
- `public PluginPickerInput()` — Initializes a new instance of the PluginPickerInput class with an empty list of picker inputs.
- `public PluginPickerInput(List<PickerInput> inputs)` — Initializes a new instance of the PluginPickerInput class which contains a predefined sequence of inputs.

### Methods
#### `public void Add(PickerInput input)`

Appends a picker input to the plug-in input.

**Parameters:**
- `input` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.PickerInput) — The picker input to append.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/2e8405d4-bef2-bbf7-c056-e1f6c63c8352)

#### `public void AddRange(List<PickerInput> inputs)`

Appends several picker inputs to the picker input sequence.

**Parameters:**
- `inputs` (System.Collections.Generic.List<PickerInput>) — All elements of the collection passed in the parameter will be appended in the same order to the plug-in input.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/e5b2b352-1609-2d51-f946-15a3d19593a8)

## PointList (class)

The PointList class defines a type safe point list.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/46ef3374-513f-103b-3d95-13a94c678f33)

### Constructors
- `public PointList()` — Initializes a new instance of the PointList class

### Methods
#### `public int Add(Point value)`

Adds a new point to the end of the point list.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point to be added to the list.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index where the point was added.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0a85dfdc-b822-8a8f-e229-363ef592a753)

#### `public void AddRange(PointList value)`

Appends all points from another point list to the end of this point list.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.PointList) — The points to be added to the list.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/99fd5ef6-df1d-d958-1935-606fee669fab)

#### `public bool Contains(Point value)`

Checks if the given point is in the list.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point to be checked.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the point is in the list, otherwise false.

Gets a part of the point list.

**Parameters:**
- `index` (System.Int32) — The start index of the new list.
- `count` (System.Int32) — The number of items in the new list.

#### `public int IndexOf(Point value)`

Gets the index of the specified point.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point to locate in the list.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index of the point if found in the list, otherwise -1.

#### `public int IndexOf(Point value, int startIndex)`

Gets the index of the specified point between the index to start the search from and the end of the list.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point to locate in the list.
- `startIndex` (System.Int32) — The index to start the search from.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index of the point if found in the list, otherwise -1.

#### `public int IndexOf(Point value, int startIndex, int count)`

Gets the index of the specified point between the index to start the search from and startIndex + count.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point to locate in the list.
- `startIndex` (System.Int32) — The index to start the search from.
- `count` (System.Int32) — The number of items searched.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index of the point if found in the list, otherwise -1.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b1382631-8c95-1737-6763-124469f375f7)

## PointPlacing (class)

The PointPlacing class defines a placing where the object is placed at one point (the insertion point) without any additional placing options.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/2b95dd6f-0941-805a-9f6b-07976e857327)

### Constructors
- `public PointPlacing()` — Creates a new point placing instance. The value used for the placing is the parent object's insertion point in this case.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/be2242f1-aa49-fbdb-fc2e-ac7b35b4f910)

## PointPlacingType (class)

The PointPlacingType class defines a placing type where the object is placed at one point (the insertion point) without any additional placing options.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a6e0ca36-fefa-9b75-fd24-f764eab84ec0)

### Constructors
- `public PointPlacingType()` — Creates a new point placing type instance.

## Polygon (class)

The Polygon class defines a polygon that is a multipoint line which is connected. It can also have a bulge (curved lines).

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/f171c5a5-0d65-7d63-7a2d-0d8428e631f8)

### Constructors
- `public Polygon(ViewBase view, PointList pointList)` — Creates a new polygon instance with the point list using standard attributes.
- `public Polygon(ViewBase view, PointList pointList, Polygon.PolygonAttributes attributes)` — Creates a new polygon instance with the point list using the given attributes.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (Polygon.PolygonAttributes, get/set) — The attributes for the polygon.
- `Bulge` (Double, get/set) — The bulge (curve) to use for the polygon (a bulge is the ratio between the height and width of an object).
- `Bulges` (List<Double>, get/set) — The list of individual bulges for the polygon.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `Points` (PointList, get/set) — The list of points for the polygon.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## Polygon.PolygonAttributes (class)

The PolygonAttributes class is the attributes class of the polygon.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/562f0e90-6ddc-aa05-5066-0a8e0e80742c)

### Constructors
- `public PolygonAttributes()` — Creates a new default polygon attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public PolygonAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new polygon attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/911a092e-34a8-d822-fc21-ffda11bcd033)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1a4be117-bc05-ff96-b7a7-b26a0989425f)

### Properties
- `BehindModelObjects` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets whether the closed graphic object is drawn behind model objects
- `Hatch` (GraphicObjectHatchAttributes, get/set) — The hatch attributes of the closed graphic object (the closed graphic object is hatchable).
- `Line` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line attributes of the graphic object. A graphic object always consists of lines.

## Polyline (class)

The Polyline class defines a polyline that is a multipoint line. It can also have a bulge (curved lines).

### Properties
- `Attributes` (Polyline.PolylineAttributes, get/set) — The attributes for the polyline.
- `Bulge` (Double, get/set) — The bulge (curve) to use for the polyline (a bulge is the ratio between the height and width of an object).
- `Bulges` (List<Double>, get/set) — The list of individual bulges for the polygon.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `Points` (PointList, get/set) — The list of points for the polyline.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

The PolylineAttributes class is the attributes class of the polyline.

## PourBreak (class)

The PourBreak class contains methods related to pour breaks. A pour break is a drawing object derived from a model object. It represents a drawing pour break that has a reference to the actual pour break in the model database.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/2edc2f18-1091-31bb-9a18-c68a6023cbb6)

## PourObject (class)

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the objects are referencing the same database object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/675fe6a8-2f1f-6e66-07f4-768bc3a1c74b)

#### `public override bool Modify()`

Calls the system to update/modify the object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/84f08d85-b6cb-0b6c-c830-17580415c3c0)

#### `public override bool Select()`

Calls the system to select and retrieve the object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/18e70902-d622-606e-70a4-07b6ff928176)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string Name, double Value)`

Sets a double property for the drawing object. The object has to be in a drawing before the method can be used.

**Parameters:**
- `Name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `Value` (System.Double) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/995715f7-c86c-86d0-359f-76dc4d14197a)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string Name, int Value)`

Sets an integer property for the drawing object. The object has to be in a drawing before the method can be used.

**Parameters:**
- `Name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `Value` (System.Int32) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/88a5c4aa-fb12-e341-ee9e-fcd3284fab68)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string Name, string Value)`

Sets a string property for the drawing object. The object has to be in a drawing before the method can be used.

**Parameters:**
- `Name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `Value` (System.String) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/633cfaf7-22e7-1bb1-6596-053d78749c52)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Changes the string presentation of the drawing object.

**Returns:** `String` — The name of the current drawing object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/23e70841-4b0d-9dc2-7a9b-f3b6d7eed19f)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (PourObject.PourAttributes, get/set) — The attributes of the pour. For more information see PourObject.PourAttributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## PourObject.PourAttributes (class)

The PourAttributes class is the attributes class for the pour.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5b4159f0-d8d8-3643-a751-bc4a891d8816)

### Constructors
- `public PourAttributes()` — Creates a new default pour attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public PourAttributes(string AttributeFile)` — Creates a new pour attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/11186dbb-413d-d09a-a4fe-e5a4173f2644)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c2ca65bc-9ea4-e06c-494a-f22dbcd642e9)

### Properties
- `DrawChamfers` (Boolean, get/set) — True if chamfers are drawn.
- `DrawHiddenLines` (Boolean, get/set) — True if hidden lines are drawn.
- `DrawOwnHiddenLines` (Boolean, get/set) — True if own hidden lines are drawn.
- `FaceHatch` (ModelObjectHatchAttributes, get/set) — The hatch type of the face hatch.
- `HiddenLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of hidden lines.
- `SectionFaceHatch` (ModelObjectHatchAttributes, get/set) — The hatch type of the section face hatch.
- `VisibleLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of visible lines.

## PreferredMarkPlacingTypes (class)

The PreferredMarkPlacingTypes class contains the available preferred mark placing types. These are allowed to be used for marks. However, there are different mark types and not all types can be used by all marks. If you try to use the wrong type it will not be accepted (Insert, Modify will fail).

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/8e8d4861-e6de-1baf-ba97-9ee03154eed1)

### Methods
#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase AlongLineOrWithLeaderLineAndParentObjectAlongPartPlacingType()`

The object is placed using the along line placing as the first option. If there is no room for the first option the fallback option will be used. The fallback option is placing the object with a leader line and rotated to be at the same angle as the connected part.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new along line or with leader line and parent object along part placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/6d3540d9-24e3-6269-a47b-001bf7d8bc4f)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase AlongLineOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType()`

The object is placed using the along line placing as the first option. If there is no room for the first option the fallback option will be used. The fallback option is placing the object with a leader line.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new along line or with leader line placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1601449f-48f3-fe2b-3370-7a3754f21808)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase AlongLinePlacingType()`

The object is placed using the along line placing.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new along line placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5f098f43-2054-cd59-c87d-4e1b4f870dee)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase AlongPartCenteredPlacingType()`

The object is placed using the along line placing. The line used will be the part's edge in the center of that line.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new along part centered placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a972a34f-74e0-7329-39e3-c9d1370159fd)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase InsidePartAlongPartOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType()`

The object is placed using the base line placing as the first option. If there is no room for the first option the fallback option will be used. The fallback option is placing the object with a leader line. The base line used is in the middle of the part, aligned with the part.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new inside part along part or with leader line placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/376611d0-8168-9b78-18f4-4d2a822964d8)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase InsidePartAlongPartPlacingType()`

The object is placed using the base line placing. The base line used is in the middle of the part, aligned with the part.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new inside part along part placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/df64f61b-ae1e-b113-57bd-64dbf50c7266)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase InsidePartHorizontalOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType()`

The object is placed using the base line placing as the first option. If there is no room for the first option the fallback option will be used. The fallback option is placing the object with a leader line. The base line used is in the middle of the part, aligned with the part. In addition the object will be rotated to be horizontal in the drawing.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new inside part horizontal or with leader line placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1eb7852e-30f6-18b3-5a18-c04ede39612c)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase InsidePartHorizontalPlacingType()`

The object is placed using the base line placing. The base line used is in the middle of the part, aligned with the part. In addition the object will be rotated to be horizontal in the drawing.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new inside part horizontal placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d10771d3-a18f-4a93-0d8f-fe215dad4d4e)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase LeaderLineAndParentObjectAlongPartPlacingType()`

The object is placed with a leader line and rotated to be at the same angle as the connected part.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new leader line and parent object along part placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9ee993af-0f5b-196f-30da-475c8ad0f470)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase LeaderLinePlacingType()`

The object is placed with a leader line.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new leader line placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0b63f11c-400b-e9ce-ad61-3211f61e4c03)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase PointPlacingType()`

The object is placed at one point (the insertion point) without any additional placing options.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new point placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/67efdc2d-3657-5658-5c5c-c58d93d569a0)

## PreferredPlacingTypeBase (class)

The PreferredPlacingTypeBase class is the base class for the preferred placing type. You can set the PreferredPlacingType in the object's attributes to specify how your object should be placed in the drawing. Please note that not all objects accept all types. If you try to use the wrong type it will not be accepted (Insert, Modify will fail). To use valid types you should use the named defaults (PreferredTextPlacingTypes for Text, PreferredMarkPlacingTypes for Mark, PreferredSymbolPlacingTypes for Symbol).

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b87c3562-b4be-a343-1589-2b1dd093c416)

## PreferredPlacingTypes (class)

The PreferredPlacingTypes class contains all the available preferred placing types. Please note that not all objects accept all types. If you try to use the wrong type it will not be accepted (Insert, Modify will fail).

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/3a23c62f-126c-7cf8-6fc8-dd677ef0e771)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase AlongLineOrWithLeaderLineAndParentObjectAlongPartPlacingType()`

The object is placed using the along line placing as the first option. If there is no room for the first option the fallback option will be used. The fallback option is placing the object with a leader line and rotated to be at the same angle as the connected part.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new along line or with leader line and parent object along part placing type instance.

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase BaseLineWithArrowAtEndPointPlacingType()`

The object is placed using the base line placing. The line is drawn and will have an arrow at the end point.

The object is placed using the base line placing. The line is drawn and will have an arrow at the start point.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new base line with arrow at start point placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a36b8468-3ccd-2124-730b-74dcc5b7e525)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase InsidePartAlongPartOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType()`

The object is placed using the base line placing as the first option. If there is no room for the first option the fallback option will be used. The fallback option is placing the object with a leader line. The base line used is in the middle of the part, aligned with the part.

**Returns:** `PreferredPlacingTypeBase` — A new inside part along part or with leader line placing type instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/dd4622b7-338d-4a27-3216-9ece3e1bf71a)

#### `public static PreferredPlacingTypeBase InsidePartHorizontalOrWithLeaderLinePlacingType()`

The object is placed using the base line placing as the first option. If there is no room for the first option the fallback option will be used. The fallback option is placing the object with a leader line. The base line used is in the middle of the part, aligned with the part. ## PrintAttributes (class)

The PrintAttributes class contains the attributes for controlling the printing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1cba3ab8-d44e-01ee-d0f2-fbab61eb2a3d)

### Constructors
- `public PrintAttributes()` — Creates a new PrintAttributes instance.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4e37a7ae-9c14-2009-7793-1a9928692792)

### Properties
- `NumberOfCopies` (Int32, get/set) — The number of copies in printing.
- `Orientation` (DotPrintOrientationType, get/set) — The print orientation type.
- `PrintArea` (DotPrintAreaType, get/set) — The print area type.
- `PrinterInstance` (String, get/set) — The name of the printer instance. Can be fetched from the printer catalog.
- `PrintToMultipleSheet` (Boolean, get/set) — Whether to print to multiple sheets.
- `Scale` (Double, get/set) — The scale factor.
- `ScalingType` (DotPrintScalingType, get/set) — The scale type.

## PropertyElement (class)

The PropertyElement class may represent any of the many different mark element types, such as the profile, the material and the name. The property is defined by the name and it is converted into a textual value before the mark is drawn.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/91af96df-1eb0-e9ac-31c9-33a3bd2abbdc)

### Constructors
- `public PropertyElement(PropertyElement.PropertyElementType PropertyType)` — Creates a new property element instance with the given property element type.

### Methods
#### `public override Object Clone()`

Clones the object.

**Returns:** `Object` — A copy of the object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a3b5281a-031c-19d2-f42b-65a64fe2d6db)

#### `public override string GetUnformattedString()`

Returns the content as a string without formatting.

**Returns:** `String` — The content as a string without formatting.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/825d769f-e545-be31-92b9-85a86bb9a3ac)

#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/db24463c-811c-73fb-56bf-2fa2c4b07f34)

### Properties
- `Font` (FontAttributes, get/set) — The font of the property.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The property name.
- `PropertyType` (PropertyElement.PropertyElementType, get) — The type of the property element.
- `Value` (String, get) — The current value of the property.

## RadiusDimension (class)

The RadiusDimension class defines a radius dimension that displays the radius of the given arc.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/cd974d85-701f-9b61-e4bb-6955d7efa816)

### Constructors
- `public RadiusDimension(ViewBase View, Point ArcPoint1, Point ArcPoint2, Point ArcPoint3, double Distance)` — Creates a new radius dimension instance using three points and the distance.
- `public RadiusDimension(ViewBase View, Point ArcPoint1, Point ArcPoint2, Point ArcPoint3, double Distance, RadiusDimensionAttributes Attributes)` — Creates a new radius dimension instance with the attributes, three points and the distance.

### Methods
#### `public override bool Delete()`

Deletes the dimension from the database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the deletion succeeds.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/35f59670-a535-3441-b6d3-bfd79adb418c)

#### `public override DimensionSetBase GetDimensionSet()`

Radius dimensions don't have dimension sets.

**Returns:** `DimensionSetBase` — An exception.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/8f7eb088-aace-b802-9371-e18d7f8f262b)

#### `public override DimensionSetBase GetDimensionSet(bool Select)`

Radius dimensions don't have dimension sets.

**Parameters:**
- `Select` (System.Boolean) — Whether to select the object from the database or not.

**Returns:** `DimensionSetBase` — An exception.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/bb7ae1f7-a0be-f198-69ca-33f905958f5d)

Retrieves all double user properties for the object with the given list of names.

**Parameters:**
- `values` (System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<String,Double>.) — Dictionary of name/value pairs that contain the values of the found user properties with the given names.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the request succeeded (the returned values can still be empty if no UserProperties were found).

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b25f0e6b-ab5c-154a-a45c-4f5868a20599)

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/7f327fc5-14b5-f534-da39-b68d3f87124a)

Changes the string presentation of the drawing object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/23e70841-4b0d-9dc2-7a9b-f3b6d7eed19f)

### Properties
- `ArcPoint1` (Point, get) — Gets the first arc point.
- `ArcPoint2` (Point, get) — Gets the second arc point.
- `ArcPoint3` (Point, get) — Gets the third arc point.
- `Attributes` (RadiusDimensionAttributes, get/set) — The radius dimension attributes.
- `Distance` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the distance.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## RadiusDimensionAttributes (class)

The RadiusDimensionAttributes class contains attributes to manage all the attributes of a straight dimension.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/63e95acf-3561-9d38-0ba6-172ff088e445)

### Constructors
- `public RadiusDimensionAttributes()` — Creates a new radius dimension attributes instance which loads standard attributes.
- `public RadiusDimensionAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new radius dimension attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/f5601fad-2842-6f5d-6f0e-a2726866153e)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads attributes from the specified attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The attribute file's filename.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/553f5f0a-be30-616c-aefc-f9e2ec0fbe85)

### Properties
- `Arrowhead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead attributes of the dimension.
- `Color` (DrawingColors, get/set) — The color of the dimension line.
- `Format` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionFormatAttributes, get/set) — The format attributes of the dimension.
- `Placing` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionPlacingAttributes, get/set) — The placing attributes of the dimension.
- `Text` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTextAttributes, get/set) — The text attributes of the dimension.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — The dimension text background transparency. (transparent/opaque)
- `TrueColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (RGB enabled) of the dimension line.

## Rectangle (class)

The Rectangle class defines a rectangle that is a box made of four lines with orthogonal angles.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/54ce7e2f-93ea-3eeb-ea30-cdc6e5bf453b)

### Constructors
- `public Rectangle(ViewBase view, Point startPoint, Point endPoint)` — Creates a new rectangle instance with two points using standard attributes. Please note that creating a rectangle using two points will re-calculate the start point to correspond with positive widths and heights.
- `public Rectangle(ViewBase view, Point startPoint, double width, double height)` — Creates a new rectangle instance with the start point and the defined width and height using standard attributes.
- `public Rectangle(ViewBase view, Point startPoint, Point endPoint, Rectangle.RectangleAttributes attributes)` — Creates a new rectangle instance with two points using the given attributes. Please note that creating a rectangle using two points will re-calculate the start point to correspond with positive widths and heights.
- `public Rectangle(ViewBase view, Point startPoint, double width, double height, Rectangle.RectangleAttributes attributes)` — Creates a new rectangle instance with the start point and the defined width and height using the given attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool Delete()`

Calls the system to delete the object.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/724d7fa7-8819-dfac-4c9c-700264d7ef79)

#### `public bool GetDoubleUserProperties(out Dictionary<string, double> values)`

Retrieves all double user properties for the object with the given list of names.

**Parameters:**
- `values` (System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<String,Double>.) — Dictionary of name/value pairs that contain the values of the found user properties with the given names.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the request succeeded (the returned values can still be empty if no UserProperties were found).

Changes the string presentation of the drawing object.

**Returns:** `String` — The name of the current drawing object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/23e70841-4b0d-9dc2-7a9b-f3b6d7eed19f)

### Properties
- `Angle` (Double, get/set) — The angle of the rectangle. The rectangle rotates around its start point.
- `Attributes` (Rectangle.RectangleAttributes, get/set) — The attributes of the rectangle.
- `EndPoint` (Point, get) — The end point of the rectangle.
- `Height` (Double, get/set) — The height of the rectangle. Changing the height changes the end point with the height.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — The start point of the rectangle. Changing this will move the rectangle.
- `Width` (Double, get/set) — The width of the rectangle. Changing the width changes the end point with the width.

## Rectangle.RectangleAttributes (class)

The RectangleAttributes class is the attributes class of the rectangle.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d916e102-2fc3-f888-8399-dc94fc4cfebf)

### Constructors
- `public RectangleAttributes()` — Creates a new default rectangle attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public RectangleAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new rectangle attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/6e4b5fa1-be4f-1840-4fd0-de810b7e10bf)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9e964ef1-34b9-4ffb-33fe-480366a83dd1)

### Properties
- `BehindModelObjects` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets whether the closed graphic object is drawn behind model objects
- `Bulge` (Double, get/set) — The bulge value for the rectangle.
- `Hatch` (GraphicObjectHatchAttributes, get/set) — The hatch attributes of the closed graphic object (the closed graphic object is hatchable).
- `Line` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line attributes of the graphic object. A graphic object always consists of lines.

## RectangleBoundingBox (class)

The RectangleBoundingBox class represents a bounding box for an object, i.e. the surrounding box of the object. The bounding box contains the calculated corner points of the box. The rectangle bounding box is inherited from the axis-aligned bounding box. The AABB also contains methods for getting the center point of the box.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/93efcf40-92db-3e7d-2cb4-5e220d916f5b)

### Methods
#### `public bool Collide(AABB Other)`

Checks if the current axis-aligned 3d bounding box collides with another given axis-aligned 3d bounding box. Both axis-aligned 3d bounding boxes need to be in the same coordinate system or in the same workplane, so that they are defined using the same axes.

**Parameters:**
- `Other` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.AABB) — Another axis-aligned 3d bounding box to be used in the collision check.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the axis-aligned 3d bounding boxes collide.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/057b4309-6593-9b8a-581e-8233518f597a)

#### `public static RectangleBoundingBox CreateRectangleBoundingBox(Point CenterPoint, double Width, double Height, double AngleToAxis)`

Creates a new RectangleBoundingBox with the given values.

**Parameters:**
- `CenterPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The center point of the bounding box.
- `Width` (System.Double) — The width of the bounding box.
- `Height` (System.Double) — The height of the bounding box.
- `AngleToAxis` (System.Double) — The angle of the box (compared to the X-axis).

**Returns:** `RectangleBoundingBox` — The created rectangle bounding box.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c57f052a-b758-e28b-434d-8090532e341b)

#### `public Point GetCenterPoint()`

Returns the geometric center point of the current axis-aligned 3d bounding box.

**Returns:** `Point` — The geometric center point of the current axis-aligned 3d bounding box.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/015fb65b-ad44-3e33-e891-0d9351e340cf)

#### `public Point[] GetCornerPoints()`

Returns all corner points of the current axis-aligned 3d bounding box.

**Returns:** `.Point.` — All corner points of the current axis-aligned 3d bounding box.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5f6f6ca4-13aa-d956-047a-f84112c028bb)

#### `public bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d4a6968c-2054-eb16-a2bb-5fc4573e2a28)

#### `public bool IsInside(LineSegment LineSegment)`

Checks if the given line segment is inside the current axis-aligned 3d bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `LineSegment` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.LineSegment) — The line segment to be checked.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the point is inside the current axis-aligned 3d bounding box.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/31e46219-4fba-b1da-4b5b-2ab276eef609)

#### `public bool IsInside(Point Point)`

Checks if the given point is inside the current axis-aligned 3d bounding box.

**Parameters:**
- `Point` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point to be checked.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the point is inside the current axis-aligned 3d bounding box.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/29c49520-1316-8e73-c29e-f4c1dd49711d)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Returns the rectangle bounding box values as a string.

**Returns:** `String` — The rectangle bounding box values as a string.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/cc7026d3-a7d7-7763-b336-e2b914e42252)

### Properties
- `AngleToAxis` (Double, get) — The angle of the bounding box in relation to the axis (the coordinate system's X-plane).
- `Height` (Double, get) — The height of the bounding box.
- `LowerLeft` (Point, get) — The lower left corner point of the bounding box.
- `LowerRight` (Point, get) — The lower right corner point of the bounding box.
- `MaxPoint` (Point, get/set) — The maximum point of the axis-aligned 3d bounding box.
- `MinPoint` (Point, get/set) — The minimum point of the axis-aligned 3d bounding box.
- `UpperLeft` (Point, get) — The upper left corner point of the bounding box.
- `UpperRight` (Point, get) — The upper right corner point of the bounding box.
- `Width` (Double, get) — The width of the bounding box.

- `Value` (System.String) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/633cfaf7-22e7-1bb1-6596-053d78749c52)

#### `public override string ToString()`

Changes the string presentation of the drawing object.

**Returns:** `String` — The name of the current drawing object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/23e70841-4b0d-9dc2-7a9b-f3b6d7eed19f)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (ReferenceModel.ReferenceModelAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the reference model object attributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## ReinforcementBase (class)

The ReinforcementBase class contains methods related to reinforcements. A reinforcement is a drawing object derived from a model object. It represents a drawing reinforcement that has a reference to the actual reinforcement in the model database.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/159fff52-3638-7a00-c559-67ae63e8a579)

### Methods
#### `public override bool Delete()`

Deletes the drawing reinforcement from the current drawing database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5417940b-7449-1720-f9d1-b0b4732c0764)

#### `public bool GetDoubleUserProperties(out Dictionary<string, double> values)`

Retrieves all double user properties for the object with the given list of names.

**Parameters:**
- `values` (System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary<String,Double>.) — Dictionary of name/value pairs that contain the values of the found user properties with the given names.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the request succeeded (the returned values can still be empty if no UserProperties were found).

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b25f0e6b-ab5c-154a-a45c-4f5868a20599)

- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.

## ReinforcementBase.HookedEndSymbolTypes (enum)

The available hooked end symbol types. See Tekla Structures help, "Reinforcement appearance", for more information.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/8e4956a2-6d10-7e8e-7aa9-fd0338c4ef15)

### Values
- `NoHook` = `1` — No hook symbol.
- `HalfHook` = `2` — The half hook symbol.
- `FullHook` = `3` — The full hook symbol.

## ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementBaseAttributes (class)

The ReinforcementBaseAttributes class is the attributes class for the reinforcement.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/184f289b-bf2a-1406-6387-c9db0de79a39)

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/787be2aa-0991-a803-fcd1-9548946d766e)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d0b91e0d-69ce-a357-cb15-716ca34687ce)

### Properties
- `CustomPresentation` (String, get/set) — Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the object type
- `HiddenLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of the hidden lines of the reinforcement.
- `HideLinesHiddenByPart` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether lines hidden by parts should be hidden or not.
- `HideLinesHiddenByReinforcement` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether lines hidden by reinforcements should be hidden or not.
- `ReinforcementRepresentation` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementRepresentationTypes, get/set) — The representation of reinforcing bars.
- `VisibleLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of the visible lines of the reinforcement.

## ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementGroupAttributes (class)

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/846ae007-a932-b8e1-933b-a423311c1f91)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d0b91e0d-69ce-a357-cb15-716ca34687ce)

### Properties
- `CustomPresentation` (String, get/set) — Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the object type
- `HiddenLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of the hidden lines of the reinforcement.
- `HideLinesHiddenByPart` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether lines hidden by parts should be hidden or not.
- `HideLinesHiddenByReinforcement` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether lines hidden by reinforcements should be hidden or not.
- `HookedEndSymbolType` (ReinforcementBase.HookedEndSymbolTypes, get/set) — Defines how the hooked ends of reinforcing bars should look like.
- `ReinforcementRepresentation` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementRepresentationTypes, get/set) — The representation of reinforcing bars.
- `ReinforcementVisibility` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementVisibilityTypes, get/set) — Defines which bars in the group appear in drawings.
- `StraightEndSymbolType` (ReinforcementBase.StraightEndSymbolTypes, get/set) — Defines how the straight ends of reinforcing bars should look like.
- `VisibleLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of the visible lines of the reinforcement.

## ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementMeshAttributes (class)

The ReinforcementMeshAttributes class is the base attributes class for ReinforcementMesh.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/cf7408d5-abf8-bc64-7087-ae2389369349)

### Constructors
- `public ReinforcementMeshAttributes()` — Creates a new default reinforcement attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public ReinforcementMeshAttributes(bool SkipLoadingAttributes)` — Creates a new default reinforcement attributes instance that loads standard attributes. If it set to true attributes won't be lodaded.
- `public ReinforcementMeshAttributes(string AttributeFile)` — Creates a new reinforcement attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/73beb5c4-187c-b116-b9d1-24415cf0bf65)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d0b91e0d-69ce-a357-cb15-716ca34687ce)

### Properties
- `CustomPresentation` (String, get/set) — Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the object type
- `HiddenLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of the hidden lines of the reinforcement.
- `HideLinesHiddenByPart` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether lines hidden by parts should be hidden or not.
- `HideLinesHiddenByReinforcement` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether lines hidden by reinforcements should be hidden or not.
- `MeshReinforcementSymbolIndex` (Int32, get/set) — Defines the index for the mesh symbol to be used. The mesh symbol appears in the middle of the mesh line when ReinforcementRepresentation == OutLine. The index starts from 0 and corresponds to the symbol in the file mesh.sym.
- `MeshReinforcementSymbolSize` (Double, get/set) — Defines the size of the reinforcement mesh symbol.
- `MeshReinforcementVisibilityCrossing` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementVisibilityTypes, get/set) — Defines which crossing bars in the mesh appear in drawings.
- `MeshReinforcementVisibilityLongitudinal` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementVisibilityTypes, get/set) — Defines which longitudinal wires in the mesh appear in drawings.
- `ReinforcementRepresentation` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementRepresentationTypes, get/set) — The representation of reinforcing bars.
- `VisibleLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of the visible lines of the reinforcement.

## ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementRepresentationTypes (enum)

The available reinforcement representation types. See Tekla Structures help, "Reinforcement appearance", for more information.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/16b9e0af-60a4-8a44-1cbf-bb7be95cc3dd)

### Values
- `SingleLine` = `1` — A single line with radiused bends.
- `DoubleLine` = `2` — The outline of a bar with radiused bends.
- `Filled` = `3` — A solid bar with radiused bends.
- `Stick` = `4` — A single line without radiused bends.
- `OutLine` = `5` — Shows the shape of the mesh using an outline rectangle or polygon, and a diagonal line. Only applies to reinforcement meshes.
- `DoubleLineFilledEnds` = `6` — The outline of a bar with radiused bends and filled bar ends.
- `SingleLineFilledEnds` = `7` — A single line with radiused bends and filled bar ends.
- `OutlineIgnoreHoles` = `8` — Shows the shape of the mesh using an outline rectangle or polygon, and a diagonal line. It ignores the holes in the meshes. Only applies to reinforcement meshes.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a5f43557-02c7-1312-8b14-e3def23c5741)

### Constructors
- `public ReinforcementStrandAttributes()` — Creates a new default reinforcement attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public ReinforcementStrandAttributes(bool SkipLoadingAttributes)` — Creates a new default reinforcement attributes instance that loads standard attributes. If it set to true attributes won't be lodaded.
- `public ReinforcementStrandAttributes(string AttributeFile)` — Creates a new reinforcement attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/e0f41474-dfc7-ca16-fde9-adcbdd9a2add)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d0b91e0d-69ce-a357-cb15-716ca34687ce)

### Properties
- `CustomPresentation` (String, get/set) — Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the object type
- `HiddenLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of the hidden lines of the reinforcement.
- `HideLinesHiddenByPart` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether lines hidden by parts should be hidden or not.
- `HideLinesHiddenByReinforcement` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether lines hidden by reinforcements should be hidden or not.
- `HookedEndSymbolType` (ReinforcementBase.HookedEndSymbolTypes, get/set) — Defines how the hooked ends of reinforcing bars should look like.
- `ReinforcementRepresentation` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementRepresentationTypes, get/set) — The representation of reinforcing bars.
- `ReinforcementVisibility` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementVisibilityTypes, get/set) — Defines which bars in the group appear in drawings.
- `StraightEndSymbolType` (ReinforcementBase.StraightEndSymbolTypes, get/set) — Defines how the straight ends of reinforcing bars should look like.
- `VisibleLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of the visible lines of the reinforcement.

## ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementVisibilityTypes (enum)

The available reinforcement visibility types. Only applies to bar groups and meshes. See Tekla Structures help, "Setting the visibility of reinforcement in drawings", for more information.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4b3131b4-003c-4de6-41ce-787cc77fb8e4)

### Values
- `All` = `1` — Shows all bars in a group or mesh.
- `First` = `2` — Only shows the first bar in the group or mesh.
- `Last` = `3` — Only shows the last bar in the group or mesh.
- `FirstAndLast` = `4` — Shows the first and last bar in the group or mesh.
- `OneInTheMiddle` = `5` — Shows one bar in the middle of the group or mesh.
- `TwoInTheMiddle` = `6` — Shows two bars in the middle of the group or mesh.
- `Customized` = `7` — Indicates that you have specified the location of the only visible reinforcing bar. See "Adjusting reinforcing bars" in the Tekla Structures help for more information.

## ReinforcementBase.StraightEndSymbolTypes (enum)

The available straight end symbol types. See Tekla Structures help, "Reinforcement appearance", for more information.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/291c577a-03d1-f01b-e1ec-64975aab8399)

### Values
- `NoSymbol` = `1` — No straight end symbol.
- `HooksOnInnerSide` = `2` — Draws half hooks on the reinforcement's inner side.
- `HooksOnOuterSide` = `3` — Draws half hooks on the reinforcement's outer side.
- `HookOnReinforcementStartOuterSide` = `4` — Draws a half hook on the reinforcement's outer side in the reinforcement's start point, and another half hook on the inner side in the end point.
- `HookOnReinforcementStartInnerSide` = `5` — Draws a half hook on the reinforcement's inner side in the reinforcement's start point, and another half hook on the outer side in the end point.
- `FullHooks` = `6` — Draws full hooks in the reinforcement's end points.

## ReinforcementGroup (class)

The ReinforcementGroup class defines a reinforcement group in the drawing.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementGroupAttributes, get/set) — The attributes of the reinforcement. For more information see ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementGroupAttributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `ReinforcementCustomPosition` (Double, get/set) — Defines the location of the reinforcement bar when using ReinforcementVisibility == ReinforcementVisibilityTypes.Customized. This location is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 and it defines the proportion from the reinforcement's start point.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementMeshAttributes, get/set) — The attributes of the reinforcement. For more information see ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementMeshAttributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `ReinforcementCustomPositionCrossing` (Double, get/set) — Defines the location of the crossing bar when using MeshReinforcementVisibilityCrossing == ReinforcementVisibilityTypes.Customized. This location is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 and it defines the proportion from the mesh's start point.
- `ReinforcementCustomPositionLongitudinal` (Double, get/set) — Defines the location of the longitudinal wire when using ReinforcementVisibility == ReinforcementVisibilityTypes.Customized. This location is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 and it defines the proportion from the reinforcement's start point.

## ReinforcementPulloutElement (class)

The ReinforcementPulloutElement class illustrates the shape and dimensions of a reinforcing bar in a drawing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/952320a4-528c-1bc2-b9da-e4eaf2c29ebd)

### Constructors
- `public ReinforcementPulloutElement()` — Creates a new reinforcement pull-out element instance.

### Methods
#### `public override Object Clone()`

Clones the object.

**Returns:** `Object` — A copy of the object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4832526c-92cd-e1e1-5081-96cb7c97339d)

#### `public override string GetUnformattedString()`

Returns the content as a string without formatting.

**Returns:** `String` — The content as a string without formatting.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/499b4a65-9122-8014-eb20-cb8c7f90d748)

#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4c93af0e-c5f6-9da0-a4ed-bfeb2dc34331)

### Properties
- `AutomaticScaling` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the pull-out is scaled automatically or to a specified value.
- `BendingAngle` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether to show the pull-out bending angles.
- `BendingRadius` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether to show the pull-out bending radii.
- `CouplersEndAnchorSymbols` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether to show coupler/anchor symbols or not.
- `CouplersScale` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the scale of coupler/anchor symbols.
- `Dimensioning` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether to show the pull-out dimensions.
- `EndSymbolType` (ReinforcementPulloutElement.EndSymbols, get/set) — Gets or sets the type of the ending marks.
- `Exaggeration` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the pull-out is exaggerated.
- `Font` (FontAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the font of the dimension.
- `RotationAxis` (ReinforcementPulloutElement.Rotation, get/set) — Gets or sets the rotation of the pull-out.
- `ScaleX` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the scaling of the pull-out in the X direction.
- `ScaleY` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the scaling of the pull-out in the Y direction.

## ReinforcementPulloutElement.EndSymbols (enum)

The shape of the bar ends in the pull-out.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/fd664343-fc64-7eb6-3d15-3d523ee0498b)

### Values
- `NONE` = `1` — Obsolete. No end symbols.
- `None` = `1` — No end symbols.
- `SINGLE` = `2` — Obsolete. A single end symbol arrow.
- `Single` = `2` — A single end symbol arrow.
- `BOTH` = `3` — Obsolete. An arrow to both directions.
- `Both` = `3` — An arrow to both directions.

## ReinforcementPulloutElement.Rotation (enum)

The rotation of the pull-out.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/406dd8c3-f13c-b1f7-f468-d4fbe369036a)

### Values
- `Automatic` = `0` — Automatic rotation.
- `Plane` = `1` — Plane rotation.
- `Global` = `2` — Global 3D rotation.

## ReinforcementSetGroup (class)

The ReinforcementSetGroup class defines a reinforcement set group in the drawing. A model rebar set generates one or more groups of rebars for drawing purposes and each group is represented in a drawing as a reinforcement set group. ModelIdentifier is always zero for the ReinforcementSetGroup.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/7a692049-f2d2-daa4-af1d-0fa3d6c7df8b)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementSetGroupAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes of the reinforcement. For more information see ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementSetGroupAttributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `ReinforcementCustomPosition` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the location of the reinforcement bar when using ReinforcementVisibility == ReinforcementVisibilityTypes.Customized. This location is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 and it defines the proportion from the reinforcement's start point.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementSingleAttributes, get/set) — The attributes of the reinforcement. For more information see ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementSingleAttributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `ReinforcementCustomPosition` (Double, get/set) — Defines the location of the reinforcement bar when using ReinforcementVisibility == ReinforcementVisibilityTypes.Customized. This location is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 and it defines the proportion from the reinforcement's start point.

## ReinforcementStrand (class)

The ReinforcementStrand class defines a reinforcement mesh in the drawing.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementStrandAttributes, get/set) — The attributes of the reinforcement. For more information see ReinforcementBase.ReinforcementStrandAttributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `ReinforcementCustomPosition` (Double, get/set) — Defines the location of the reinforcement bar when using ReinforcementVisibility == ReinforcementVisibilityTypes.Customized. This location is a value between 0.0 and 1.0 and it defines the proportion from the reinforcement's start point.

## ReportTemplateElement (class)

The ReportTemplateElement class represents a template in the Tekla Structures template library.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0a6690c4-2eca-a4d2-427f-e044282fc4c3)

### Constructors

Clones the object.

## ScalingOptions (enum)

The options for controlling the scaling of the objects in the drawing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9665449d-ff04-7985-ad81-52330c936e19)

### Values
- `NoScaling` = `0` — Does not scale the object.
- `ScaleToFit` = `4` — Scales the object to fit in the frame in the Y direction.

## SectionMark (class)

The SectionMark class defines a drawing object that illustrates a straight section in a specific view.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/85ff4eb8-4c7e-c72f-4de6-59843ac0138a)

### Constructors
- `public SectionMark(View view, Point leftPoint, Point rightPoint)` — Creates a new section mark instance with the given positions.
- `public SectionMark(View view, Point leftPoint, Point rightPoint, SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkAttributes sectionMarkAttributes)` — Creates a new section mark instance with the given positions and attributes.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkAttributes, get/set) — The attributes for the section mark.
- `LeftPoint` (Point, get) — The starting point of the left symbol of the section mark.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `RightPoint` (Point, get) — The starting point of the right symbol of the section mark.

## SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkAttributes (class)

The SectionMarkAttributes class is the attributes class for section marks.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/bc3ac46a-11ed-0b50-182d-2f8fbddb2763)

### Constructors
- `public SectionMarkAttributes()` — Creates a new default SectionMarkAttributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public SectionMarkAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new SectionMarkAttributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/6e982ee0-53e8-1f44-d19b-5c335d6e4462)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string attributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `attributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a6910abe-d01a-b867-6c65-66f7f1f34fa9)

### Properties
- `LeftSymbol` (SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkSymbol, get/set) — The symbol to use on the left side of the section mark.
- `LineColor` (DrawingColors, get/set) — The color of the section mark line (see DrawingColors for options).
- `LineLength` (Double, get/set) — The length of the section mark lines.
- `LineLengthOffset` (Double, get/set) — The offset from the end of the line on both sides of the section mark.
- `LineWidth` (Double, get/set) — The width of the section mark lines.
- `LineWidthOffsetLeft` (Double, get/set) — The offset from the start of the line on the left side of the section mark.
- `LineWidthOffsetRight` (Double, get/set) — The offset from the start of the line on the right side of the section mark.
- `MarkName` (String, get/set) — The section mark name.
- `RightSymbol` (SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkSymbol, get/set) — The symbol to use on the right side of the section mark.
- `SymbolColor` (DrawingColors, get/set) — The color of the mark symbols (see DrawingColors for options).
- `TagsAttributes` (SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkTagsAttributes, get/set) — The tags A1-A5 in the section mark attributes.
- `TrueLineColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (rgb enabled) of the line.
- `TrueSymbolColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (rgb enabled) of the symbol.

## SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkSymbol (class)

The SectionMarkSymbol class is for section mark attributes related to the shape of the connecting symbol.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b08c53a3-44f2-e297-c1ab-1c5b86fa7110)

### Constructors
- `public SectionMarkSymbol()` — Creates a new section mark symbol instance.
- `public SectionMarkSymbol(SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkSymbol.SymbolShapes shape, double size, Point position)` — Creates a new section mark symbol instance with the given shape, size and position.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/109f4eec-fc10-4b25-930b-3d95fb9d173b)

### Properties
- `Position` (Point, get/set) — The position of the section mark symbol.
- `Shape` (SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkSymbol.SymbolShapes, get/set) — The shape of the section mark symbol.
- `Size` (Double, get/set) — The size of the section mark symbol.

## SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkSymbol.SymbolShapes (enum)

The possible shapes of the section mark symbol.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0a5890e1-e6db-7652-7fad-2dbfd157e0fa)

### Values
- `None` = `1` — No symbol.
- `SymbolShape1` = `101` — The triangle with black and white halves.
- `SymbolShape2` = `102` — The arrow line pointing to an orthogonal straight line.
- `SymbolShape3` = `103` — The line with half of an arrow end.
- `SymbolShape4` = `104` — The rectangle shape with very small height.
- `SymbolShape5` = `105` — The triangle and circle shapes mixed. The circle partly hides the triangle.
- `SymbolShape6` = `106` — The triangle and circle shapes mixed. The triangle is on top.
- `SymbolShape7` = `107` — The triangle shape without content.
- `SymbolShape8` = `108` — The triangle shape filled in black.
- `Custom` = `4` — The custom shape.

## SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkTagAttributes (class)

The SectionMarkTagAttributes class contains attributes for section mark tags.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/36943ef9-e37b-1238-0997-d786e733b781)

### Constructors
- `public SectionMarkTagAttributes()` — Creates a new section mark tag attributes instance.
- `public SectionMarkTagAttributes(SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkTagAttributes.TagShowOnSide showOnSide, TagLocation location, Vector offset, SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkTagAttributes.TagTextRotation rotation, ContainerElement tagContent)` — Creates a new section mark tag attributes instance with the given information.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/6fc31695-b20b-b0f1-010f-0627515c22eb)

### Properties
- `Location` (TagLocation, get/set) — The location of the mark tag.
- `Offset` (Vector, get/set) — The offset of the mark tag.
- `ShowOnSide` (SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkTagAttributes.TagShowOnSide, get/set) — The side(s) on which the tag is shown.
- `TagContent` (ContainerElement, get/set) — The tag content.
- `TagRotation` (SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkTagAttributes.TagTextRotation, get/set) — The policy for the tag rotation.

## SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkTagAttributes.TagShowOnSide (enum)

The values that determine on which side(s) the mark tag text is shown.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/600a0cdd-702c-b984-b35e-5310be105fad)

### Values
- `ShowOnBothSides` = `1` — Show on both sides.
- `ShowOnLeftSideOnly` = `2` — Show only on the left side.
- `ShowOnRightSideOnly` = `3` — Show only on the right side.

## SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkTagAttributes.TagTextRotation (enum)

The mark tag text rotation values.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a8e4a0bf-8d73-5f8a-1cf5-3f86921a4d8e)

### Values
- `AlwaysRotate` = `1` — Always rotate.
- `AlwaysHorizontal` = `2` — Always horizontal.
- `DoNotRotateVertically` = `3` — Do not rotate vertically.
- `AlwaysOrthogonal` = `4` — Always orthogonal.

## SinglePartDrawing (class)

The SinglePartDrawing class is for handling single part drawings.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a20d3504-8756-95d2-a1e5-e4813005771d)

### Constructors
- `public SinglePartDrawing(Identifier partIdentifier)` — Instantiates a new single part drawing with standard attributes.
- `public SinglePartDrawing(Identifier partIdentifier, int sheetNumber)` — Instantiates a new single part drawing with standard attributes and with a specified sheet number.
- `public SinglePartDrawing(Identifier partIdentifier, string AttributeFile)` — Instantiates a new single part drawing with given attributes.
- `public SinglePartDrawing(Identifier partIdentifier, int sheetNumber, string AttributeFile)` — Instantiates a new single part drawing with standard attributes and with a specified sheet number.

### Methods
#### `public bool CommitChanges()`

Commits the changes made to the drawings database so far. Commit inserts a kind of save-point into the database and the save-point will be retrieved if the user does undo. Commit also executes all messages. Please note that you need to also to save the drawings database to persists the changes to disk.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/bc26a611-bd6a-535a-4461-943b8bf5351c)

#### `public bool CommitChanges(string Message)`

Commits the changes made to the drawings database so far. Commit inserts a kind of save-point into the database and the save-point will be retrieved if the user does undo. Commit also executes all messages. Please note that you need to also to save the drawings database to persists the changes to disk.

**Parameters:**
- `Message` (System.String) — The message to be saved with the commit.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/92109855-5313-7446-bef4-ab6dde6875f1)

Updates the drawing object in the database.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0e24ea81-402d-8821-e2d9-86c95a78c834)

#### `public bool PlaceViews()`

Places views on the sheet. Computes the required size of the views to fit the parts, then places the views so that they fit on the sheet and do not overlap. Adjusts the sheet size if needed.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1e1578e4-084f-21a9-3d1a-2b03140440ce)

#### `public override bool Select()`

Selects the drawing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True on success.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/cd990fe6-97b8-366f-859d-d80435f7bc96)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string Name, double Value)`

Sets a double property for the drawing object. The object has to be in a drawing before the method can be used.

**Parameters:**
- `Name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `Value` (System.Double) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.
