#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string Name, int Value)`

Sets an integer property for the drawing object. The object has to be in a drawing before the method can be used.

**Parameters:**
- `Name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `Value` (System.Int32) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/88a5c4aa-fb12-e341-ee9e-fcd3284fab68)

#### `public bool SetUserProperty(string Name, string Value)`

Sets a string property for the drawing object. The object has to be in a drawing before the method can be used.

**Parameters:**
- `Name` (System.String) — The name of the property to be set.
- `Value` (System.String) — The new value for the property.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the property was set successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/633cfaf7-22e7-1bb1-6596-053d78749c52)

### Properties
- `CommitMessage` (String, get) — Gets the commit message
- `CreationDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing creation date.
- `DrawingTypeStr` (String, get) — The type of the drawing
- `IsFrozen` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is frozen.
- `IsIssued` (Boolean, get) — Describes whether the drawing is issued.
- `IsIssuedButModified` (Boolean, get) — Describes whether the drawing is issued but modified since it was issued.
- `IsLocked` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is locked.
- `IsLockedBy` (String, get) — Gets the logged in username that set the drawing to locked
- `IsMasterDrawing` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is a master drawing (shown with a special symbol on the drawing list).
- `IsReadyForIssue` (Boolean, get/set) — Describes whether the drawing is ready for issue.
- `IsReadyForIssueBy` (String, get) — Gets the logged in username that set the drawing Ready for issue
- `IssuingDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing issuing date.
- `Layout` (LayoutAttributes, get) — Gets the drawing layout attributes.
- `Mark` (String, get) — The drawing mark.
- `ModificationDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing modification date.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the drawing.
- `OutputDate` (DateTime, get) — The drawing output date. XS_DISABLE_DRAWING_PLOT_DATE controls whether this date is set.
- `PartIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The identifier of the model object part.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `SectionViewStartLabel` (String, get) — Gets the drawing attribute for start label of section view
- `SheetNumber` (Int32, get) — The sheet number of the drawing.
- `Title1` (String, get/set) — The first drawing title.
- `Title2` (String, get/set) — The second drawing title.
- `Title3` (String, get/set) — The third drawing title.
- `UpToDateStatus` (DrawingUpToDateStatus, get) — Gets the drawing up to date status.

## Size (class)

The Size class contains the width and height properties.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/7a8f20c3-67f0-3571-d11c-1ca129c00738)

### Constructors
- `public Size()` — Creates a new size instance with zero width and height.
- `public Size(double Width, double Height)` — Creates a new size instance with the specified width and height.

### Methods
#### `public override string ToString()`

Returns a string that represents this instance (in fractional inches if advanced option XS_USE_ONLY_INCHES_IN_SHEET_SIZES set to true).

**Returns:** `String` — A string that represents this instance using default separator character "x".

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1ef52bd4-9074-7210-56ff-6c3cf681ca06)

#### `public string ToString(IFormatProvider formatProvider, string separator = null)`

Returns a string that represents this instance (in fractional inches if advanced option XS_USE_ONLY_INCHES_IN_SHEET_SIZES set to true).

**Parameters:**
- `formatProvider` (System.IFormatProvider) — The format provider.
- `separator` (System.String) — The optional separator characters(s) - defaults to "x".

**Returns:** `String` — A string that represents this instance.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1b4b2d55-5e98-707a-b193-91ecb9428738)

#### `public string ToString(string separator)`

Returns a string that represents this instance (in fractional inches if advanced option XS_USE_ONLY_INCHES_IN_SHEET_SIZES set to true).

**Parameters:**
- `separator` (System.String) — The separator characters(s).

**Returns:** `String` — A string that represents this instance using the specified separator character(s).

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9731ad65-0f62-db52-cba4-b742413b369b)

### Properties
- `Height` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the height value of the size instance.
- `Width` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the width value of the size instance.

## SizeDefinitionMode (enum)

Controls the size definition mode of the drawing sheet. Set the mode to SizeDefinitionMode.SpecifiedSize if you want to force the sheet to a certain size.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/ac0235fb-263e-d1b5-334b-283dec46145e)

### Values
- `AutoSize` = `0` — The smallest possible drawing sheet is automatically calculated.
- `SpecifiedSize` = `1` — Fixed values are used to control the sheet size.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (StraightDimension.StraightDimensionAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the dimension's attributes.
- `Distance` (Double, get) — Gets or sets the distance of the dimension line from the start point of the dimension.
- `EndPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the end point of the dimension.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `StartPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the start point of the dimension.
- `UpDirection` (Vector, get/set) — Gets or sets the direction from the dimension points to the dimension line.
- `Value` (ContainerElement, get) — Gets the content of the dimension mark.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (StraightDimensionSet.StraightDimensionSetAttributes, get/set) — The straight dimension set attributes.
- `Distance` (Double, get/set) — The distance from the first dimension point to the dimension line. The distance is measured in paper millimeters.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `LeftTagLineOffset` (Double, get/set) — The offset of the left tag line. The offset is measured in paper millimeters.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `RightTagLineOffset` (Double, get/set) — The offset of the right tag line. The offset is measured in paper millimeters.

## StraightDimensionSet.StraightDimensionSetAttributes (class)

The StraightDimensionSetAttributes class contains the attributes for managing all the attributes of the straight dimension.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/f7cd1b5b-2dba-2eee-cf5d-198239da028b)

### Constructors
- `[ObsoleteAttribute("This constructor is obsolete and may be removed in the future. Instead, use the constructor which takes a ModelObject.", false)] public StraightDimensionSetAttributes()` — Creates a default straight dimension set attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `[ObsoleteAttribute("This constructor is obsolete and may be removed in the future. Instead, use the constructor which takes a ModelObject and a string.", false)] public StraightDimensionSetAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a straight dimension set attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.
- `public StraightDimensionSetAttributes(ModelObject modelObject)` — Creates a default straight dimension set attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public StraightDimensionSetAttributes(ModelObject modelObject, string AttributesFile)` — Creates a straight dimension set attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1af5065a-8f4d-e2a0-196f-d21a07480b5e)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string attributeFilename)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `attributeFilename` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9aa1604e-5949-3a5a-2c9e-85648d2384b4)

### Properties
- `Arrowhead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead attributes of the dimension.
- `Color` (DrawingColors, get/set) — The color of the dimension line.
- `CombinedDimension` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.CombinedDimensionAttributes, get/set) — Combines the attributes of the dimension.
- `DimensionType` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTypes, get/set) — The dimension type.
- `Exaggeration` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionExaggerationAttributes, get/set) — The exaggeration attributes of the dimension.
- `ExcludePartsAccordingToFilter` (String, get/set) — Sets the filter to use for excluding parts from the tags. You may query the possible filters using StraightDimensionSet.GetAllExcludePartsAccordingToFilter()
- `ExtensionLine` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.ExtensionLineTypes, get/set) — The extension line presentation of the dimension.
- `Format` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionFormatAttributes, get/set) — The format attributes of the dimension.
- `IncludePartCountInTag` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets whether the part count is included in at least one of the tag position, or set part count flag for all tag positions.
- `IncludePartCountInTagA` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the part count is included in the tag A.
- `IncludePartCountInTagB` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the part count is included in the tag B.
- `IncludePartCountInTagC` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the part count is included in the tag C.
- `IncludePartCountInTagD` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the part count is included in the tag D.
- `IncludePartCountInTagE` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the part count is included in the tag E.
- `IncludePartCountInTagF` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the part count is included in the tag F.
- `IncludePartCountInTagG` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the part count is included in the tag G.
- `LeftLowerTag` (ContainerElement, get/set) — The left lower dimension tag.
- `LeftMiddleTag` (ContainerElement, get/set) — The left middle dimension tag.
- `LeftUpperTag` (ContainerElement, get/set) — The left upper dimension tag.
- `Placing` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionPlacingAttributes, get/set) — The placing attributes of the dimension.
- `RightLowerTag` (ContainerElement, get/set) — The right lower dimension tag.
- `RightMiddleTag` (ContainerElement, get/set) — The right middle dimension tag.
- `RightUpperTag` (ContainerElement, get/set) — The right upper dimension tag.
- `ShortDimension` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.ShortDimensionTypes, get/set) — How short dimensions are shown.
- `Text` (DimensionSetBaseAttributes.DimensionTextAttributes, get/set) — The text attributes of the dimension.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — The dimension text background transparency. (transparent/opaque)
- `TrueColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (RGB enabled) of the dimension line.

## StraightDimensionSetHandler (class)

The StraightDimensionSetHandler class contains operations for creating and modifying straight dimension sets.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/13408e9f-2759-ac34-b4b2-30597ce5e73f)

### Constructors
- `public StraightDimensionSetHandler()` — Initializes a new instance of the StraightDimensionSetHandler class

### Methods
#### `public StraightDimensionSet CreateDimensionSet(ViewBase View, PointList DimensionPoints, Vector UpDirection, double Distance)`

Creates straight dimensions and combines them to one straight dimension set. Returns null if fails.

**Parameters:**
- `View` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.ViewBase) — The view to be used.
- `DimensionPoints` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.PointList) — The dimension points to be used.
- `UpDirection` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Vector) — The direction from the dimension points to the dimension line.
- `Distance` (System.Double) — The distance in paper millimeters taken from the first dimension point to the dimension line.

**Returns:** `StraightDimensionSet` — The created straight dimension set or null if fails.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/506ac2af-0156-0c5f-3531-063cbe11a51d)

#### `public StraightDimensionSet CreateDimensionSet(ViewBase View, PointList DimensionPoints, Vector UpDirection, double Distance, double LeftTagLineOffset, double RightTagLineOffset)`

Creates straight dimensions and combines them to one straight dimension set. Returns null if fails.

**Parameters:**
- `View` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.ViewBase) — The view to be used.
- `DimensionPoints` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.PointList) — The dimension points to be used.
- `UpDirection` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Vector) — The direction from the dimension points to the dimension line.
- `Distance` (System.Double) — The distance in paper millimeters taken from the first dimension point to the dimension line.
- `LeftTagLineOffset` (System.Double) — The left tag line offset measured in paper millimeters.
- `RightTagLineOffset` (System.Double) — The right tag line offset measured in paper millimeters.

**Returns:** `StraightDimensionSet` — The created straight dimension set or null if fails.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/56bf2ebf-e514-45d2-121c-c41d1f8644b0)

Creates straight dimensions and combines them to one straight dimension set. Returns null if fails.

**Parameters:**

- `Distance` (System.Double) — The distance in paper millimeters taken from the first dimension point to the dimension line.

## StringList (class)

The StringList class defines a type safe string collection.

### Constructors
- `public StringList()` — Creates a new string list instance.
- `public StringList(int Capacity)` — Creates a new string list instance with the given capacity.

### Methods

Adds a new string to the end of the string list.

**Parameters:**
- `value` (System.String) — The string to be added to the list.

**Returns:** `Int32` — The index where the string was added.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/fc0068a0-ffb4-23a5-8a95-e02ec5836e20)

Gets a part of the string list.

- `value` (System.String) — The string to locate in the list.
- `startIndex` (System.Int32) — The index to start the search from.

Gets the index of the specified string between the index to start the search from and startIndex + count.

- `value` (System.String) — The string to locate in the list.
- `startIndex` (System.Int32) — The index to start the search from.

## Surfacing (class)

The Surfacing class contains methods related to surfacings. A surfacing is a drawing object derived from a model object. It represents a drawing surfacing that has a reference to the actual surfacing in the model database.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/95313b14-458b-8b48-d281-5cca62d1122a)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (Surfacing.SurfacingAttributes, get/set) — The attributes of the surfacing. For more information see Surfacing.SurfacingAttributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## Surfacing.Representation (enum)

The surfacing representations.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/12021b92-4283-acf8-69db-3025a78584fa)

### Values
- `Outline` = `1` — The outline representation.
- `Symbol` = `2` — The symbol representation.
- `WorkshopForm` = `4` — The workshop form representation.
- `Exact` = `8` — The exact representation.
- `BoundingBox` = `16` — The bounding box representation.
- `BaseBox` = `32` — The base box representation.

## Surfacing.SurfacingAttributes (class)

The SurfacingAttributes class is the attributes class for the surfacing.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b0b71097-ef0e-95a2-5a44-398c01b6f009)

### Constructors
- `public SurfacingAttributes()` — Creates a new default surfacing attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public SurfacingAttributes(string AttributeFile)` — Creates a new surfacing attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/4b4ee56c-622d-74d8-99f1-bcf616d3fb67)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/53def60f-8695-9925-0755-4fc288c1bb65)

### Properties
- `DrawHiddenLines` (Boolean, get/set) — True if hidden lines are drawn.
- `DrawOwnHiddenLines` (Boolean, get/set) — True if own hidden lines are enabled.
- `HiddenLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of hidden lines.
- `Representation` (Surfacing.Representation, get/set) — The representation type of the surfacing.
- `ShowPattern` (Boolean, get/set) — True if the pattern is shown.
- `VisibleLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of visible lines.

## Symbol (class)

The Symbol class defines a drawing object that is displayed as a symbol in a drawing. Symbols are located in the environment directory under the folder named "symbols" in .sym files that can contain up to 255 symbols. Symbol files can be created with SymEd. See the Tekla Structures help for more information.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/dc665636-f4f7-758f-1e69-414de20de61c)

### Constructors
- `public Symbol(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint)` — Creates a new symbol instance using the default symbol information and standard attributes.
- `public Symbol(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, PlacingBase Placing)` — Creates a new symbol instance using the default symbol information, standard attributes and the given placing.
- `public Symbol(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, SymbolInfo SymbolInfo)` — Creates a new symbol instance using standard attributes.
- `public Symbol(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, SymbolInfo SymbolInfo, PlacingBase Placing)` — Creates a new symbol instance using standard attributes.
- `public Symbol(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, SymbolInfo SymbolInfo, Symbol.SymbolAttributes Attributes)` — Creates a new symbol instance using the given attributes.
- `public Symbol(ViewBase View, Point InsertionPoint, SymbolInfo SymbolInfo, Symbol.SymbolAttributes Attributes, PlacingBase Placing)` — Creates a new symbol instance using the given attributes and placing.

### Properties
- `Attributes` (Symbol.SymbolAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the symbol attributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `InsertionPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the symbol insertion point.
- `Placing` (PlacingBase, get/set) — The current placing of the object. See the placing types for different placing options.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `SymbolInfo` (SymbolInfo, get/set) — Gets or sets the symbol information.

## Symbol.SymbolAttributes (class)

The SymbolAttributes class contains the attributes for the symbol.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b10faf4e-f57b-f8ce-e4b7-6127b94600e7)

### Constructors
- `public SymbolAttributes()` — Creates a new default symbol attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public SymbolAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a symbol attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/6ccd8468-c5e8-7d1e-0de9-78423f575142)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string attributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `attributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/7bbea6d2-d775-8e1a-ad4b-4fae564d6b74)

### Properties
- `Angle` (Double, get/set) — The angle of the symbol. Cannot be changed for an editor-created symbol when the symbol is created "along line".
- `Color` (DrawingColors, get/set) — The symbol color. Does not have an effect on symbols where the color is already defined in the symbol file.
- `Frame` (Frame, get/set) — The frame for the symbol.
- `Height` (Double, get/set) — The height of the symbol.
- `PreferredPlacing` (PreferredPlacingTypeBase, get/set) — The type of placing the object should use. See the preferred placing types for different types of placing.
- `TrueColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (RGB enabled) of the symbol. Does not have an effect on symbols where the color is already defined in the symbol file.

## SymbolElement (class)

The SymbolElement class represents a symbol in the Tekla Structures symbol library.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/ec0e7aaa-df5c-48a8-bded-69228d9b9b11)

### Constructors
- `public SymbolElement()` — Creates a new symbol element instance with the default symbol.
- `public SymbolElement(SymbolInfo Symbol)` — Creates a new symbol element instance with the given symbol.
- `public SymbolElement(SymbolInfo Symbol, DrawingColors Color, double Height)` — Creates a new symbol element instance with the given symbol, color and height.
- `public SymbolElement(SymbolInfo Symbol, TeklaDrawingColor Color, double Height)` — Creates a new symbol element instance with the given symbol, the RGB color and height.

### Methods
#### `public override Object Clone()`

Clones the object.

**Returns:** `Object` — A copy of the object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/1d7480df-5842-f41e-3eac-d5a8d5570b7b)

- `SymbolIndex` (Int32, get/set) — Gets or sets the the symbol index. Throws an ArgumentOutOfRangeException if the symbol index is not in the range from 0 to 255.

## TagLocation (enum)

The mark tag location values.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/cf4a5079-333d-3f02-24ca-d86be19552f1)

### Values
- `AboveLine` = `1` — The mark tag is above the line.
- `BelowLine` = `2` — The mark tag is below the line.
- `MiddleLevelOfSymbol` = `3` — The mark tag is in the middle level of the mark symbol.
- `AboveSymbolCenterLine` = `4` — The mark tag is above the center line of the mark symbol.
- `BelowSymbolCenterLine` = `5` — The mark tag is below the center line of the mark symbol.
- `CustomRelativeToSymbol` = `6` — The mark tag is in a custom location relative to the mark symbol.

## TeklaDrawingColor (struct)

The TeklaDrawingColor struct contains a compact storage for drawing color data This struct is intended to be an "all-in-one" solution for OpenAPI users interacting with drawing colors. It supports old enums and new user defined RGB colors. The essential principle of this struct is that once an object of this struct is created (i.e. you have one of these objects instantiated), it contains valid RGB information, as well as a valid enum value in the case of a standard color. This means users will never have to run checks to see if their object has a valid RGB value. After all, if they receive this "TrueColor" object, they expect we can provide them RGB information. To support this functionality, we need to make sure that there is RGB information present for every single object of this type that is created. All objects constructed with RGB values are guaranteed to have this, as well as the ordinary standard drawing colors with values between 0 and 255. Other indices are forbidden, and will cause an error upon construction attempt. Given that the constructor of this struct can throw an exception, the struct should never be used as a "middleman", meaning it should never be used to pass color information from one place to another within the codebase: int color -> TeklaDrawingColor -> int color.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/6e7f1080-8544-0c94-f532-8ab243698da9)

### Constructors
- `public TeklaDrawingColor()` — Default constructor for the struct. Assigns color to standard black.
- `public TeklaDrawingColor(Color RGB)` — Construct a TeklaDrawingColor instance by passing in an RGB System.Drawing.Color value. This is the safe non-throwing constructor to use.
- `public TeklaDrawingColor(DrawingColors ColorEnum)` — Construct a TeklaDrawingColor instance by passing in a Tekla standard color enum value. Use of this constructor is discouraged, please use TeklaDrawingColor(Color RGB)
- `public TeklaDrawingColor(DrawingHatchColors ColorEnum)` — Construct a TeklaDrawingColor instance by passing in a Tekla standard color hatch enum value. Use only for hatches.

### Properties
- `DrawingColorEnum` (DrawingColors, get) — Enum for the drawing color
- `RGBColor` (Color, get) — Red, Green, Blue representation of the color.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

### Properties
- `Alignment` (TextAlignment, get/set) — The text object's alignment (left, center, right).
- `Angle` (Double, get/set) — The angle of the text frame.
- `ArrowHead` (ArrowheadAttributes, get/set) — The arrowhead of the leader line.
- `CustomPresentation` (String, get/set) — Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the object type
- `Font` (FontAttributes, get/set) — The text object's font settings.
- `Frame` (Frame, get/set) — The frame for the text.
- `PlacingAttributes` (PlacingAttributes, get/set) — The placing attributes that the object should use. Using these you can specify whether the object is automatically arranged in the drawing or not.
- `PreferredPlacing` (PreferredPlacingTypeBase, get/set) — The type of placing the object should use. See the preferred placing types for different types of placing.
- `RulerWidth` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the width in paper coordinates of the ruler to be used in word wrapping.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the background transparency is enabled for texts.
- `UseWordWrapping` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether to [use word wrapping].

## TextFile (class)

The TextFile class defines a text file object in a drawing.

**Remarks:** A text file object can be inserted as-is without scaling or it can be forced to fit inside the given space. The content is scaled in the Y direction, but it can overflow in the X direction.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d6c4338c-95d8-e22d-c7cd-44d9ca5daef5)

- `public TextFile(ViewBase view, Point insertionPoint, string fileName, TextFile.TextFileAttributes attributes)` — Creates a new text file instance using specific attributes.
- `public TextFile(ViewBase view, Point insertionPoint, string fileName, Size size, TextFile.TextFileAttributes attributes)` — Creates a new text file instance using specific attributes.

### Properties
- `Angle` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the angle (in degrees).
- `Attributes` (TextFile.TextFileAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the text file attributes.
- `FileName` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the file name defining the embedded object.
- `InsertionPoint` (Point, get/set) — Gets or sets the insertion point.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `Size` (Size, get/set) — Gets or sets the size of the frame of the embedded object.

## TextFile.TextFileAttributes (class)

The TextFileAttributes class contains the attributes for the text file object.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c547587c-07dc-1a38-ccf3-592042bf2205)

### Constructors
- `public TextFileAttributes()` — Creates a new text file attributes instance that loads standard attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c631729a-82b1-c6c1-ed34-e077d86a0b6d)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the specified attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The name of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if succeeded.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/ffaa1763-eb69-ab96-d557-fb060ce8b690)

### Properties
- `Font` (FontAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the font.
- `Frame` (EmbeddedObjectFrame, get/set) — Gets or sets the frame.
- `Line` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the line type.
- `LineType` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — Obsolete.
- `Scaling` (ScalingOptions, get/set) — Gets or sets the scaling.
- `XScale` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the X scaling value. Changing this property will set the scaling type to ScaleXY.
- `YScale` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the Y scaling value. Changing this property will set the scaling type to ScaleXY.

## UnitAttributes (class)

The UnitAttributes class handles attributes related to units and formatting of values.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/7c59d106-b059-1d79-d697-33133265f971)

### Constructors
- `public UnitAttributes()` — Creates a new default UnitAttributes attributes instance. The default values are Units.Automatic, FormatTypes.Automatic.
- `public UnitAttributes(Units unit, FormatTypes format)` — Creates a new UnitAttributes attributes instance with the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public Object Clone()`

Clones the object.

**Returns:** `Object` — A copy of the object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a99f0c77-a698-90f2-307d-556ec55a9881)

#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/e5dea66a-b241-1e2a-6275-085f3184c73d)

### Properties
- `Format` (FormatTypes, get/set) — Gets or sets the format to use.
- `Unit` (Units, get/set) — Gets or sets the unit to use.

## Units (enum)

The available unit types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/a050ba82-07ea-f06f-3467-eaab7b462ca5)

### Values
- `Automatic` = `0` — Automatic, chooses the most appropriate unit for the current environment.
- `Millimeters` = `1` — Show the value in millimeters.
- `Centimeters` = `2` — Show the value in centimeters.
- `Meters` = `3` — Show the value in meters.
- `FeetAndInches` = `4` — Show the value in feet and inches.
- `CentimetersOrMeters` = `5` — Show the value in centimeters or meters whichever is more appropriate.
- `Inches` = `6` — Show the value in inches.
- `Feet` = `7` — Show the value in feet.

## UserDefinedElement (class)

The UserDefinedElement class represents a user-defined attribute specified by its name. A user-defined element is converted into a textual value before it is drawn.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d2294ddd-0810-2f6c-e414-9e64867bc2e1)

### Constructors
- `public UserDefinedElement(string Name)` — Creates a new user-defined element instance with the given name.
- `public UserDefinedElement(string Name, FontAttributes Font)` — Creates a new user-defined element instance with the given name and font.

### Methods
#### `public override Object Clone()`

Clones the object.

**Returns:** `Object` — A copy of the object.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9d8c81d8-26c1-6ef9-1130-663591375ba2)

#### `public override string GetUnformattedString()`

Returns the content as a string without formatting.

**Returns:** `String` — The content as a string without formatting.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/2471ba47-3a7d-b112-a1f7-1999248324b3)

#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/7267437d-5400-eab8-2d2c-0f7bb7aacea1)

### Properties
- `Font` (FontAttributes, get/set) — The font of the user-defined attribute.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the user-defined attribute.
- `Unit` (UnitAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the unit attributes.
- `Value` (String, get) — The value of the user-defined attribute.

## View (class)

The View class contains methods related to views. Views are views that contain other drawing objects, excluding other views. Views also contain drawing representations of model objects.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/c677c912-dc7d-0e16-cc44-433195eee9d2)

### Constructors
- `public View(ContainerView View, CoordinateSystem ViewCoordinateSystem, CoordinateSystem DisplayCoordinateSystem, ArrayList PartList)` — Creates a new view instance with the given coordinate systems and part list.
- `public View(ContainerView View, CoordinateSystem ViewCoordinateSystem, CoordinateSystem DisplayCoordinateSystem, AABB RestrictionBox)` — Creates a new view instance with the given coordinate systems and model area.
- `public View(ContainerView View, CoordinateSystem ViewCoordinateSystem, CoordinateSystem DisplayCoordinateSystem, ArrayList PartList, string Attributes)` — Creates a new view instance with the given coordinate systems, part list and attributes file's filename.
- `public View(ContainerView View, CoordinateSystem ViewCoordinateSystem, CoordinateSystem DisplayCoordinateSystem, AABB RestrictionBox, string Attributes)` — Creates a new view instance with the given coordinate systems, model area and attributes file's filename.

### Methods
#### `public static bool Create3dView(Drawing drawing, CoordinateSystem viewPlane, CoordinateSystem displayPlane, AABB viewExtrema, Point viewInsertionPoint, View.ViewAttributes viewAttributes, out View view)`

Generates a new 3d view on the sheet of the given active drawing using given view plane and display plane.

**Parameters:**
- `drawing` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.Drawing) — The active drawing in which to create the view.
- `viewPlane` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.CoordinateSystem) — The view plane.
- `displayPlane` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.CoordinateSystem) — The display plane of the view.
- `viewExtrema` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.AABB) — The restriction box of the view.
- `viewInsertionPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The center point of the view.
- `viewAttributes` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.ViewAttributes) — The attributes for the view to be created.
- `view` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.) — The created view is returned if the creation was successful. Otherwise, null is returned.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the view was created successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/55ff2f8d-a8ab-0d68-0d86-92bb423cb8b5)

#### `public static bool Create3dView(Drawing drawing, Point viewInsertionPoint, View.ViewAttributes viewAttributes, out View view)`

Generates a new 3d view on the sheet of the given active drawing.

**Parameters:**
- `drawing` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.Drawing) — The active drawing in which to create the view.
- `viewInsertionPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The center point of the view.
- `viewAttributes` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.ViewAttributes) — The attributes for the view to be created.
- `view` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.) — The created view is returned if the creation was successful. Otherwise, null is returned.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the view was created successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/b76cddf7-6db5-800f-66f1-9c0e522c6753)

- `drawing` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.Drawing) — The active drawing in which to create the view.

- `viewAttributes` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.ViewAttributes) — The attributes for the view to be created.
- `view` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.) — The created view is returned if the creation was successful. Otherwise, null is returned.

- `viewAttributes` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.ViewAttributes) — The attributes for the view to be created.

#### `public static bool CreateCurvedSectionView(View view, Point startPoint, Point middlePoint, Point endPoint, Point viewInsertionPoint, double depthUp, double depthDown, View.ViewAttributes viewAttributes, SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkAttributes sectionMarkAttributes, out View curvedSectionView, out CurvedSectionMark sectionMark)`

Generates a new curved section view on the sheet of the active drawing and the associated section mark for the specified view.

**Parameters:**
- `view` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View) — The view in which to place the section mark.
- `startPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The starting point of the section mark.
- `middlePoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The middle point of the section mark.
- `endPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The ending point of the section mark.
- `viewInsertionPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point where to create the section view.
- `depthUp` (System.Double) — The view depth up that the section view should cover.
- `depthDown` (System.Double) — The view depth down that the section view should cover.
- `viewAttributes` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.ViewAttributes) — The attributes for the section view to be created.
- `sectionMarkAttributes` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkAttributes) — The attributes for the section mark to be created.
- `curvedSectionView` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.) — The created section view is returned if the creation was successful. Otherwise, null is returned.
- `sectionMark` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.CurvedSectionMark.) — The created section mark is returned if the creation was successful. Otherwise, null is returned.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the section view was created successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/96f0d083-d484-cea9-3fc0-e534f94a4fd1)

#### `public static bool CreateDetailView(View view, Point centerPoint, Point boundaryPoint, Point labelPoint, Point viewInsertionPoint, View.ViewAttributes viewAttributes, DetailMark.DetailMarkAttributes detailMarkAttributes, out View detailView, out DetailMark detailMark)`

Generates a new detail view on the sheet of the active drawing and the associated detail mark for the specified view.

- `centerPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The center point of the detail mark.
- `boundaryPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The boundary point of the detail mark.
- `labelPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The label point of the detail mark label.
- `viewInsertionPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point where to create the detail view.
- `viewAttributes` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.ViewAttributes) — The attributes for the detail view to be created.
- `detailMarkAttributes` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.DetailMark.DetailMarkAttributes) — The attributes for the detail mark to be created.
- `detailView` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.) — The created detail view is returned if the creation was successful. Otherwise, null is returned.
- `detailMark` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.DetailMark.) — The created detail mark is returned if the creation was successful. Otherwise, null is returned.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the detail view was created successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/39279a5c-e5a3-ea02-8bdc-c8d8c7c3cea2)

#### `public static bool CreateSectionView(View view, Point startPoint, Point endPoint, Point viewInsertionPoint, double depthUp, double depthDown, View.ViewAttributes viewAttributes, SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkAttributes sectionMarkAttributes, out View sectionView, out SectionMark sectionMark)`

Generates a new section view on the sheet of the active drawing and the associated section mark for the specified view.

**Parameters:**
- `view` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View) — The view in which to place the section mark.
- `startPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The starting point of the section mark.
- `endPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The ending point of the section mark.
- `viewInsertionPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The point where to create the section view.
- `depthUp` (System.Double) — The view depth up that the section view should cover.
- `depthDown` (System.Double) — The view depth down that the section view should cover.
- `viewAttributes` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.ViewAttributes) — The attributes for the section view to be created.
- `sectionMarkAttributes` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.SectionMarkBase.SectionMarkAttributes) — The attributes for the section mark to be created.
- `sectionView` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.) — The created section view is returned if the creation was successful. Otherwise, null is returned.
- `sectionMark` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.SectionMark.) — The created section mark is returned if the creation was successful. Otherwise, null is returned.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the section view was created successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/524fe216-b878-33be-8db7-035a7da252f1)

#### `public static bool CreateTopView(Drawing drawing, Point viewInsertionPoint, View.ViewAttributes viewAttributes, out View view)`

Generates a new top view on the sheet of the given active drawing.

**Parameters:**
- `drawing` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.Drawing) — The active drawing in which to create the view.
- `viewInsertionPoint` (Tekla.Structures.Geometry3d.Point) — The center point of the view.
- `viewAttributes` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.ViewAttributes) — The attributes for the view to be created.
- `view` (Tekla.Structures.Drawing.View.) — The created view is returned if the creation was successful. Otherwise, null is returned.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the view was created successfully.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5893bec2-ade0-9949-35de-f2f52db65e9d)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (View.ViewAttributes, get/set) — The view attributes.
- `DisplayCoordinateSystem` (CoordinateSystem, get/set) — The coordinate system of the display plane. This can be used to transform global points from the model that is to be inserted in the view.
- `ExtremaCenter` (Point, get) — Gets the view extrema center.
- `FrameOrigin` (Vector, get/set) — The vector from the view origin to the frame origin.
- `Height` (Double, get/set) — The height of the view frame in paper coordinates.
- `IsSheet` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the container view is the drawing's sheet.
- `Name` (String, get/set) — The name of the view.
- `Origin` (Point, get/set) — The view origin coordinates in the sheet.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `RestrictionBox` (AABB, get/set) — The axis aligned bounding box that represents the volume of the view.
- `ViewCoordinateSystem` (CoordinateSystem, get/set) — The coordinate system of the view plane.
- `ViewType` (View.ViewTypes, get) — The view type information.
- `Width` (Double, get/set) — The width of the view frame in paper coordinates.

## View.HorizontalLabelPosition (enum)

The horizontal label position types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/2b927757-222e-2465-f880-2410862df005)

### Values
- `CenteredByViewFrame` = `1` — The label is centered by the view frame.
- `CenteredByViewRestrictionBox` = `2` — The label is centered by the view restriction box.
- `LeftAlignedByViewFrame` = `3` — The label is left aligned by the view frame.
- `RightAlignedByViewFrame` = `4` — The label is right aligned by the view frame.
- `LeftAlignByViewRestrictionBox` = `5` — The label is left aligned by the view restriction box.
- `RightAlignByViewRestrictionBox` = `6` — The label is right aligned by the view restriction box.

## View.LabelLineLengthType (enum)

The label line length types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/96db0a18-547a-5b83-dadc-dd300725426f)

### Values
- `Minimal` = `1` — The label is of minimal possible length according to the label text.
- `Custom` = `2` — The label is of custom length.

## View.ShorteningCutPartType (enum)

The vertical label position types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/bdeb3e98-0558-a3d5-d224-2b8f0a6eb242)

### Values
- `BothDirections` = `1` — The shortening is done to both x and y directions.
- `X_Direction` = `2` — The shortening is done only to x direction.
- `Y_Direction` = `3` — The shortening is done only to y direction.

## View.VerticalLabelPosition (enum)

The vertical label position types.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/0f88254d-66dc-5106-8c22-03dbfd891b58)

### Values
- `Top` = `1` — The label is located on the top of the view.
- `Bottom` = `2` — The label is located on the bottom of the view.

## View.ViewAttributes (class)

The ViewAttributes class contains the attributes for the view.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/37966d35-61b2-f198-c207-c709508a943a)

### Constructors
- `public ViewAttributes()` — Creates a new view attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public ViewAttributes(string AttributesFile)` — Creates a new view attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/88f7ad53-6e09-7bf7-e523-1b30b1fc152b)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string attributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `attributeFile` (System.String) — The attribute file's filename.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9e31a4e7-f908-e01d-d4b0-99f0582dc4f6)

### Properties
- `DatumLevel` (Double, get/set) — The specified datum level. If ViewPlaneDatumPointForElevations is set to true when DatumLevel is set, the value of ViewPlaneDatumPointForElevations will change to false.
- `FixedViewPlacing` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the view placing is fixed (true) or free (false).
- `LabelPositionHorizontal` (View.HorizontalLabelPosition, get/set) — The horizontal position of the view label.
- `LabelPositionVertical` (View.VerticalLabelPosition, get/set) — The vertical position of the view label.
- `LocationBy` (String, get/set) — Gets or sets the location by attribute. Value is ViewAttributes.LocationByModelOrigin, ViewAttributes.LocationByProjectBasePoint or guid of a Basepoint.
- `MarkSymbolAttributes` (View.ViewMarkSymbolAttributes, get/set) — The view mark symbol attributes.
- `MarkSymbolColor` (DrawingColors, get/set) — The color of the mark symbol (see DrawingColors for options).
- `PartialProfileLength` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the partial profile length.
- `PartialProfileOffset` (Double, get/set) — Gets or sets the partial profile offset
- `PourView` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the view is pour view or not.
- `ReflectedView` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the view is reflected or not.
- `Scale` (Double, get/set) — The view scale.
- `Shortening` (View.ViewShorteningAttributes, get/set) — Gets or sets the shortening attributes for the view.
- `ShowPartOpeningsOrRecessSymbol` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether to show the openings/recess symbol or not.
- `TagsAttributes` (View.ViewMarkTagsAttributes, get/set) — The attributes for view mark tags.
- `TrueMarkSymbolColor` (TeklaDrawingColor, get/set) — The true color (rgb enabled) of the mark symbol.
- `UndeformedView` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the view is undeformed or not.
- `UnfoldedView` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the view is unfolded or not.
- `ViewExtensionForNeighbourParts` (Double, get/set) — The view extension for neighbour parts.
- `ViewPlaneDatumPointForElevations` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether the datum point for elevations is view plane based (true) or specified (false). When set to false, the value of DatumLevel is used. When set to true, DatumLevel will be set to 0.0.

## View.ViewMarkSymbolAttributes (class)

The ViewMarkSymbolAttributes class contains symbol attributes for view mark tags.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/2db7869f-e77d-a9a1-c2fa-f1f737208f33)

- `public ViewMarkSymbolAttributes(double size, MarkSymbolShape shape, View.LabelLineLengthType lineLengthType, double lineLength)` — Creates a new mark symbol attributes instance with the given size, shape, line length type and line length.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/55a27e06-da69-f6ab-6337-1b71216f247d)

### Properties
- `LineLength` (Double, get/set) — The label line length in the case of custom length.
- `LineLengthType` (View.LabelLineLengthType, get/set) — The label line length type.
- `Shape` (MarkSymbolShape, get/set) — The shape of the mark symbol.
- `Size` (Double, get/set) — The size of the mark symbol.

## View.ViewMarkTagsAttributes (class)

The ViewMarkTagsAttributes class contains attributes for view mark tags.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9066f94e-5bf0-17fa-79ab-5e44c3c90ba7)

### Constructors
- `public ViewMarkTagsAttributes()` — Creates a new mark tags attributes instance.
- `public ViewMarkTagsAttributes(View.ViewMarkTagAttributes tagA1, View.ViewMarkTagAttributes tagA2, View.ViewMarkTagAttributes tagA3, View.ViewMarkTagAttributes tagA4, View.ViewMarkTagAttributes tagA5)` — Creates a new mark tags attributes instance.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/5d087974-45d9-02b4-ea01-e21fa5153ffd)

### Properties
- `TagA1` (View.ViewMarkTagAttributes, get/set) — The tag A1 of a mark.
- `TagA2` (View.ViewMarkTagAttributes, get/set) — The tag A2 of a mark.
- `TagA3` (View.ViewMarkTagAttributes, get/set) — The tag A3 of a mark.
- `TagA4` (View.ViewMarkTagAttributes, get/set) — The tag A4 of a mark.
- `TagA5` (View.ViewMarkTagAttributes, get/set) — The tag A5 of a mark.

## View.ViewShorteningAttributes (class)

The ViewShorteningAttributes class sets the shortening attributes for the view. If CutParts is set to true, the other values are applied. If it is false, none of the other values matter.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/17b1c7e5-f3c2-f128-ba48-d25e5c1618b4)

### Constructors
- `public ViewShorteningAttributes()` — Creates a new default view shortening attributes instance.
- `public ViewShorteningAttributes(bool CutParts, bool CutSkewParts, double MinimumLength, double Offset, View.ShorteningCutPartType CutPartType = View.ShorteningCutPartType.BothDirections)` — Creates a new view shortening attributes instance with the given parameters.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/d5db5013-c321-f2b7-34fe-17024157b078)

### Properties
- `CutParts` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether cut parts are enabled (true) or disabled (false).
- `CutPartType` (View.ShorteningCutPartType, get/set) — Defines cut part type. Not used if CutParts is false.
- `CutSkewParts` (Boolean, get/set) — Defines whether cut skewed parts are enabled (true) or disabled (false).
- `MinimumLength` (Double, get/set) — The minimum cut part length.
- `Offset` (Double, get/set) — The space between the cut parts.

## View.ViewTypes (enum)

The possible types of views.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/e2c4286c-cf79-8fb8-cad3-db5fa4ea7030)

### Values
- `UnknownViewType` = `0` — The view type is not known.
- `FrontView` = `1` — Front side view of the part.
- `TopView` = `2` — Top side view of the part.
- `BackView` = `3` — Back side view of the part.
- `BottomView` = `4` — Bottom side view of the part.
- `EndView` = `5` — End side view of the part.
- `SectionView` = `6` — Section view of the part.
- `ModelView` = `7` — Model view of the part.
- `DetailView` = `8` — Detail view of the part.
- `_3DView` = `9` — 3d view of the part.

## ViewBase (class)

### Properties
- `Attributes` (AttributesBase, get/set) — Gets or sets the attributes of the current object. Only attributes of the same type as the object are allowed.
- `ExtremaCenter` (Point, get) — Gets the view extrema center.
- `FrameOrigin` (Vector, get/set) — The vector from the view origin to the frame origin.
- `Height` (Double, get/set) — The height of the view frame in paper coordinates.
- `IsSheet` (Boolean, get) — Gets a value indicating whether the container view is the drawing's sheet.
- `Origin` (Point, get/set) — The view origin coordinates in the sheet.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).
- `Width` (Double, get/set) — The width of the view frame in paper coordinates.

## Weld (class)

The Weld class represents weldings in drawing views that refer to actual welds in the model database. It has properties and methods to control the graphical representation of welding lines. Instances of Weld class cannot be inserted with code. These appear in drawings when views are created/updated. To modify its graphical representation, attributes of Weld class can use .welo setting files.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/8e62e9d2-d28d-f0e5-c421-9e0a8cd05a97)

- `Attributes` (Weld.WeldAttributes, get/set) — The attributes of the weld. For more information see Weld.WeldAttributes.
- `Hideable` (Hideable, get) — Accesses the information if this object is hidden or not or if it should be.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — The database identifier of the model object in the model database. By using this identifier, it is possible to select the actual object in the Tekla Structures model.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

- `Path` = `2` — The path representation.

## Weld.WeldAttributes (class)

The WeldAttributes class is the attributes class for the weld.

- `DrawHiddenLines` (Boolean, get/set) — True if hidden lines are drawn.
- `DrawOwnHiddenLines` (Boolean, get/set) — True if own hidden lines are drawn.
- `HiddenLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of hidden lines.
- `Representation` (Weld.Representation, get/set) — The representation of the weld.
- `VisibleLines` (LineTypeAttributes, get/set) — The line type of visible lines.

## WeldMark (class)

The WeldMark class defines a drawing object that illustrates a weld mark in a specific view. WeldMark class refers to welding marks that appear when creating/updating drawing views. In this case, ModelIdentifier has a value. WeldMark class also refers to weld marks created with drawing editor UI. In this case, ModelIdentifier value is zero. Graphical representation of welding marks (line and text properties) can be modified using .wel setting files. The welding mark settting files (.wls) cannot be used to modify instances of WeldMark class.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/dae803f9-fb07-c6d7-ad0f-df44a120a6e4)

Deletes the weld mark from the current drawing database.

- `InsertionPoint` (Point, get/set) — The insertion point of the mark.
- `ModelIdentifier` (Identifier, get) — When instance refers to welding mark related to a weld in model database, ModelIdentifier has a value. When instance refers to weld mark created with drawing editor UI, value is zero.
- `QueryReturnValue` (ReturnValuesEnum, get/set) — Status information about the latest database operation (select, insert, modify, delete).

## WeldMark.SeamVisibilityAttributes (class)

The SeamVisibilityAttributes class is the attributes class for the weld seam visibility.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/853a2750-92ba-db30-78e3-543bbe6914d1)

### Constructors
- `public SeamVisibilityAttributes()` — Creates a new weld seam visibility attributes instance.
- `public SeamVisibilityAttributes(bool showPrefix, bool showSize, bool showType, bool showAngle, bool showContour, bool showFinish, bool showLength, bool showPitch, bool showEffectiveThroat, bool showRootOpening)` — Creates a new weld seam visibility attributes instance with the given attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/e1d49834-4506-32f5-7fe4-0dab996df6af)

### Properties
- `ShowAngle` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the seam angle is visible.
- `ShowContour` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the seam contour is visible.
- `ShowEffectiveThroat` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the seam effective throat is visible.
- `ShowFinish` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the seam finish is visible.
- `ShowLength` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the seam length is visible.
- `ShowPitch` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the seam pitch is visible.
- `ShowPrefix` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the seam prefix is visible.
- `ShowRootOpening` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the seam root opening is visible.
- `ShowSize` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the seam size is visible.
- `ShowType` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets a value indicating whether the seam type is visible.

## WeldMark.WeldMarkAttributes (class)

The WeldMarkAttributes class is the attributes class for the weld mark attributes.

[Vendor docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/98722d9e-764f-601c-d375-077400a3052c)

### Constructors
- `public WeldMarkAttributes()` — Creates a new default weld mark attributes instance that loads standard attributes.
- `public WeldMarkAttributes(string AttributeFile)` — Creates a new weld mark attributes instance that loads the specified attributes.

### Methods
#### `public override bool IsEqual(Object ObjectToCompare)`

Compares the current object with an object of the same type.

**Parameters:**
- `ObjectToCompare` (System.Object) — The object to be used for comparing.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if all the fields (except for the database GUID) of the objects match.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/15f7400e-a8c0-6cef-0991-aa99d4daa923)

#### `public override bool LoadAttributes(string AttributeFile)`

Loads the attributes from the attribute file.

**Parameters:**
- `AttributeFile` (System.String) — The filename of the attribute file.

**Returns:** `Boolean` — True if the attributes are loaded correctly. False if the attribute file loading failed.

[Docs](https://developer.tekla.com/topic/en/18/43/9f018710-63bd-27fd-fa0f-8e8e34de85ab)

### Properties
- `CustomPresentation` (String, get/set) — Custom presentation GUID to be applied to the object type
- `Font` (FontAttributes, get/set) — The weld mark font settings.
- `SeamAbove` (WeldMark.SeamVisibilityAttributes, get/set) — The visibility of the seam above parameters.
- `SeamBelow` (WeldMark.SeamVisibilityAttributes, get/set) — The visibility of the seam below parameters.
- `ShowEdgeAround` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets visibility of the weld mark edge/around property.
- `ShowReferenceText` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets visibility of the weld mark reference text.
- `ShowWeldNumber` (Boolean, get/set) — The visibility of the weld number.
- `ShowWorkshopSite` (Boolean, get/set) — Gets or sets visibility of the weld mark workshop/site property.
- `TransparentBackground` (Boolean, get/set) — Controls the text background transparency (transparent/opaque).
